 1227 return lookup

 1230 + def _load_nces_names () -> tuple :

 1231 + """Load canonical district and school names from NCES territory data.

 1232 + Returns (district_names: dict[name_key → canonical_name],

 1233 + school_to_district: dict[school_name_key → district_name],

 1234 + district_set: set[canonical_name])

 1235 + """

 1236 + from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials

 1237 + from googleapiclient.discovery import build as gapi_build

 1238 +

 1239 + creds_json = os.environ.get( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON" )

 1240 + territory_id = os.environ.get( "GOOGLE_SHEETS_TERRITORY_ID" )

 1241 + if not creds_json or not territory_id:

 1242 + return {}, {}, set ()

 1243 +

 1244 + creds = Credentials.from_service_account_info(

 1245 + json.loads(creds_json),

 1246 + scopes = [ "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets.readonly" ],

 1247 + )

 1248 + service = gapi_build( "sheets" , "v4" , credentials = creds)

 1249 +

 1250 + district_names = {} # normalized key → canonical name

 1251 + district_set = set ()

 1252 + school_to_district = {} # normalized school key → canonical district name

 1253 +

 1254 + # Load districts

 1255 + try :

 1256 + result = service.spreadsheets().values().get(

 1257 + spreadsheetId = territory_id, range = "'Territory Districts'!A:B"

 1258 + ).execute()

 1259 + for row in result.get( "values" , [])[ 1 :]:

 1260 + if len (row) >= 2 :

 1261 + name = row[ 1 ].strip()

 1262 + key = re.sub( r ' [ ^ a-z0-9] ' , '' , name.lower())

 1263 + district_names[key] = name

 1264 + district_set.add(name)

 1265 + except Exception :

 1266 + pass

 1267 +

 1268 + # Load schools with their parent districts

 1269 + try :

 1270 + result = service.spreadsheets().values().get(

 1271 + spreadsheetId = territory_id, range = "'Territory Schools'!A:C"

 1272 + ).execute()

 1273 + for row in result.get( "values" , [])[ 1 :]:

 1274 + if len (row) >= 3 :

 1275 + school = row[ 1 ].strip()

 1276 + district = row[ 2 ].strip()

 1277 + key = re.sub( r ' [ ^ a-z0-9] ' , '' , school.lower())

 1278 + school_to_district[key] = district

 1279 + except Exception :

 1280 + pass

 1281 +

 1282 + return district_names, school_to_district, district_set

 1283 +

 1284 +

 1285 + def normalize_names ( leads : list [ dict ]) -> list [ dict ]:

 1286 + """Normalize Company Name, District Name, School Name across all leads.

 1287 +

 1288 + Strategy:

 1289 + 1. Load NCES canonical names as ground truth

 1290 + 2. Group leads by email domain

 1291 + 3. For each domain group, determine the canonical district name:

 1292 + a. Match against NCES district names

 1293 + b. Match against DOMAIN_TO_DISTRICT_NAME map

 1294 + c. Pick the most complete/common name among leads in the group

 1295 + 4. For school-level leads, set School Name = company, District Name = canonical district

 1296 + 5. For district-level leads, set Company Name = District Name = canonical district

 1297 + """

 1298 + logger.info( " Loading NCES territory data for name normalization..." )

 1299 + nces_districts, nces_school_to_district, nces_district_set = _load_nces_names()

 1300 + logger.info( f " NCES: { len (nces_districts)} districts, { len (nces_school_to_district)} schools" )

 1301 +

 1302 + def _normalize_key ( name ):

 1303 + return re.sub( r ' [ ^ a-z0-9] ' , '' , name.lower())

 1304 +

 1305 + def _find_nces_district ( name ):

 1306 + """Try to match a name against NCES district data."""

 1307 + if not name:

 1308 + return ""

 1309 + key = _normalize_key(name)

 1310 + # Exact match

 1311 + if key in nces_districts:

 1312 + return nces_districts[key]

 1313 + # Try with common suffixes stripped

 1314 + for suffix in [ "elementaryschooldistrict" , "schooldistrict" , "unifiedschooldistrict" ,

 1315 + "unionhighschooldistrict" , "jointunionhighschooldistrict" ,

 1316 + "unionschooldistrict" , "highschooldistrict" , "cityschooldistrict" ,

 1317 + "countyofficeofeducation" , "unified" , "usd" , "esd" , "hsd" ]:

 1318 + if key.endswith(suffix):

 1319 + base = key[: - len (suffix)]

 1320 + # Search for any NCES district containing this base

 1321 + for nk, nv in nces_districts.items():

 1322 + if nk.startswith(base) and len (base) >= 4 :

 1323 + return nv

 1324 + # Substring match — find NCES district where the key is a significant part

 1325 + if len (key) >= 6 :

 1326 + for nk, nv in nces_districts.items():

 1327 + if key in nk or nk in key:

 1328 + return nv

 1329 + return ""

 1330 +

 1331 + def _find_nces_school ( name ):

 1332 + """Try to match a name against NCES school data, return parent district."""

 1333 + if not name:

 1334 + return ""

 1335 + key = _normalize_key(name)

 1336 + if key in nces_school_to_district:

 1337 + return nces_school_to_district[key]

 1338 + # Partial match

 1339 + if len (key) >= 8 :

 1340 + for sk, district in nces_school_to_district.items():

 1341 + if key in sk or sk in key:

 1342 + return district

 1343 + return ""

 1344 +

 1345 + def _is_school_name ( name ):

 1346 + """Check if a name refers to an individual school (not a district)."""

 1347 + nl = name.lower()

 1348 + # Individual school indicators

 1349 + school_indicators = [

 1350 + "elementary school" , "middle school" , "high school" , "junior high" ,

 1351 + "charter school" , "academy" , "preparatory" , "magnet" ,

 1352 + "montessori" , "k-8" , "k8" , "tk-8" , "tk8" ,

 1353 + "intermediate" , "preschool" , "day school" ,

 1354 + ]

 1355 + # District indicators take priority

 1356 + district_indicators = [

 1357 + "unified" , "school district" , "district" , "county office" ,

 1358 + "superintendent" , "office of education" ,

 1359 + ]

 1360 + for d in district_indicators:

 1361 + if d in nl:

 1362 + return False

 1363 + for s in school_indicators:

 1364 + if s in nl:

 1365 + return True

 1366 + # "X Elementary" without "District"

 1367 + if re.search( r ' \b ( elementary | middle | high | junior ) \b ' , nl) and "district" not in nl:

 1368 + return True

 1369 + return False

 1370 +

 1371 + # Group leads by email domain

 1372 + domain_groups = {}

 1373 + for lead in leads:

 1374 + if "@" not in lead[ "Email" ]:

 1375 + continue

 1376 + domain = lead[ "Email" ].split( "@" )[ - 1 ].lower()

 1377 + if domain in _GENERIC_DOMAINS :

 1378 + continue

 1379 + domain_groups.setdefault(domain, []).append(lead)

 1380 +

 1381 + normalized_count = 0

 1382 +

 1383 + for domain, group in domain_groups.items():

 1384 + # Collect all company names in this domain group

 1385 + names = [l[ "Company Name" ] for l in group if l[ "Company Name" ]]

 1386 + if not names:

 1387 + continue

 1388 +

 1389 + # Step 1: Try DOMAIN_TO_DISTRICT_NAME map

 1390 + root = extract_domain_root(group[ 0 ][ "Email" ])

 1391 + canonical_district = DOMAIN_TO_DISTRICT_NAME.get(root, "" )

 1392 +

 1393 + # Step 2: Try NCES match on each company name

 1394 + if not canonical_district:

 1395 + for name in names:

 1396 + match = _find_nces_district(name)

 1397 + if match:

 1398 + canonical_district = match

 1399 + break

 1400 +

 1401 + # Step 3: Try NCES match on the domain root itself (e.g. "compton" → Compton USD)

 1402 + if not canonical_district and root:

 1403 + for nk, nv in nces_districts.items():

 1404 + if nk.startswith(root) and len (root) >= 4 :

 1405 + canonical_district = nv

 1406 + break

 1407 +

 1408 + # Step 4: Pick the longest/most complete name among leads

 1409 + if not canonical_district:

 1410 + # Filter to district-like names (not individual schools)

 1411 + district_names_in_group = [n for n in names if not _is_school_name(n)]

 1412 + if district_names_in_group:

 1413 + canonical_district = max (district_names_in_group, key = len )

 1414 + elif names:

 1415 + canonical_district = max (names, key = len )

 1416 +

 1417 + if not canonical_district:

 1418 + continue

 1419 +

 1420 + # Apply canonical district to all leads in this domain group

 1421 + for lead in group:

 1422 + company = lead[ "Company Name" ]

 1423 +

 1424 + if _is_school_name(company):

 1425 + # This lead is at a specific school — keep school name, set district

 1426 + if not lead[ "School Name" ]:

 1427 + lead[ "School Name" ] = company

 1428 + if not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1429 + lead[ "District Name" ] = canonical_district

 1430 + lead[ "Company Name" ] = company # keep the school name as company

 1431 + if not lead[ "Org Type" ]:

 1432 + lead[ "Org Type" ] = "school"

 1433 + else :

 1434 + # District-level or unknown — normalize to canonical district name

 1435 + if company != canonical_district:

 1436 + lead[ "Company Name" ] = canonical_district

 1437 + normalized_count += 1

 1438 + if not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1439 + lead[ "District Name" ] = canonical_district

 1440 + if not lead[ "Org Type" ]:

 1441 + lead[ "Org Type" ] = "district"

 1442 +

 1443 + logger.info( f " Normalized {normalized_count} company names to canonical district names" )

 1444 + return leads

 ❯ I have to spot check unfortunately.

 I see emails with this domain @monet.k12.ca.us that should be "Modesto City Schools" why is it labeled Davis?

 I see palmdalesd.org emails that should be Palmdale School District. Why is ist labed Dale?

 I see CUSD 300 that should likely be unabbreviated and normalized to whatever it would be in NCES or the master list data, something like "Community Unit

 School District 300". Or am I worong here? Should it just be CUSD 300?

 I see wwisdom@egusd.net that should be "Elk Grove Unified School District" Why did it get normalized with emails from different domains? The others

 @richgrove.org should be at "Richgrove School District".

 I see some that say Kern High but it sholud be "Kern High School District"

 I see @slane.k12.or.us listed as Lane but it should be "South Lane School District"

 I see Mdusd but that should be "Mt. Diablo Unified School District"

 I see nlmusd but it should be "Norwalk-La Mirada Unified School District"

 I see @nvside.org but it should be "Nevada Society of Innovators and Digital Educators"

 I see pcoe but it should be "Placer County Office of Education (PCOE)"

 I see emails with "@pusd.us" and they are marked as Pomona but should be "Pasadena Unified School District"

 I see emails with "salinascityesd.org" and its labeled "Salina" but it should be "Salinas City Elementary School District"

 I see Sdusd but it should be "San Diego Unified School District"

 I see Sjusd but it should be San Jose Unified School District

 I see @srvusd.net but it should be "San Ramon Valley Unified School District"

 I see "@bhuhsd.k12.ca.us" that should be "Bret Harte Union High School District" but its listed as union with a bunch of other differnt emails.

 I see @auesd.org and its marked as union but should be Armona Union Elementary School District.

 I see @janesvilleschool.org that should be "Janesville Elementary School"

 I see @mvla.net but it should be Mountain View Los Altos High School District

 There are some HUGE mistakes in here (such as all the ones marked pomona)

 ⏺ You're right, these are serious bugs. The root causes:

 1. Aggressive substring matching — my _find_nces_district does if key in nk or nk in key which matches "monet" to something containing "one", "palmdalesd"

 to "dale", etc. This is terrible.

 2. Domain root collision — pusd.org (Pomona) and pusd.us (Pasadena) both have root "pusd", so they get the same mapping.

 3. Short matches — "bhuhsd" matching "union", "auesd" matching "union" from NCES substring.

 The fix: kill the aggressive substring matching entirely, use full domain (not just root) for the mapping, and add the missing districts.

 1260 + def _resolve_remaining_states ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 1261 + """Final pass: resolve ALL remaining US leads without a state using Serper + Claude."""

 1262 + import anthropic

 1263 + from concurrent.futures import Thread Pool Executor, as_completed

 1264 + import httpx

 1265 +

 1266 + serper_key = os.environ.get( "SERPER_API_KEY" , "" )

 1267 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1268 + if not serper_key or not api_key:

 1269 + logger.warning( " Missing SERPER_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — skipping" )

 1270 + return

 1271 +

 1272 + no_state = [l for l in leads if l.get( "Country" ) == "US" and not l.get( "State" )]

 1273 + if not no_state:

 1274 + logger.info( " No remaining US leads without state" )

 1275 + return

 1276 +

 1277 + logger.info( f " { len (no_state)} US leads still without state" )

 1278 +

 1279 + def _serper_search ( query ):

 1280 + try :

 1281 + with httpx.Client( timeout = 10.0 ) as http:

 1282 + resp = http.post(

 1283 + "https://google.serper.dev/search" ,

 1284 + headers = { "X-API-KEY" : serper_key, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 1285 + json = { "q" : query, "num" : 3 },

 1286 + )

 1287 + if resp.status_code != 200 :

 1288 + return ""

 1289 + data = resp.json()

 1290 + parts = []

 1291 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 3 ]:

 1292 + parts.append( f " {item.get( 'title' , '' )} | {item.get( 'snippet' , '' )} | {item.get( 'link' , '' )} " )

 1293 + kg = data.get( "knowledgeGraph" , {})

 1294 + if kg:

 1295 + attrs = kg.get( "attributes" , {})

 1296 + addr = attrs.get( "Address" , "" ) or attrs.get( "Headquarters" , "" )

 1297 + parts.append( f "[KG] {kg.get( 'title' , '' )} | {kg.get( 'description' , '' )} | {addr} " )

 1298 + return " \n " .join(parts) if parts else ""

 1299 + except Exception :

 1300 + return ""

 1301 +

 1302 + # Build search queries for each lead

 1303 + queries = {} # lead index → query

 1304 + for i, lead in enumerate (no_state):

 1305 + email = lead[ "Email" ]

 1306 + domain = email.split( "@" )[ - 1 ].lower() if "@" in email else ""

 1307 + company = lead.get( "Company Name" , "" )

 1308 + name = f " {lead.get( 'First Name' , '' )} {lead.get( 'Last Name' , '' )} " .strip()

 1309 + title = lead.get( "Title" , "" )

 1310 +

 1311 + is_generic = domain in _GENERIC_DOMAINS

 1312 +

 1313 + if not is_generic:

 1314 + # Institutional: search domain + company name if available

 1315 + if company:

 1316 + queries[i] = f " {company} {domain} "

 1317 + else :

 1318 + queries[i] = domain

 1319 + elif company:

 1320 + # Generic email + company: search company + name

 1321 + queries[i] = f '" {company} " {name} '

 1322 + elif name and title:

 1323 + # Generic email, no company: search name + title + educator

 1324 + queries[i] = f '" {name} " {title} educator school'

 1325 + elif name:

 1326 + queries[i] = f '" {name} " educator school district'

 1327 +

 1328 + logger.info( f " Searching { len (queries)} leads..." )

 1329 +

 1330 + # Parallel Serper search

 1331 + search_results = {}

 1332 + with Thread Pool Executor( max_workers = 20 ) as executor:

 1333 + future_map = {executor.submit(_serper_search, q): i for i, q in queries.items()}

 1334 + for future in as_completed(future_map):

 1335 + idx = future_map[future]

 1336 + search_results[idx] = future.result() or ""

 1337 +

 1338 + got = sum ( 1 for v in search_results.values() if v)

 1339 + logger.info( f " Got results for {got} / { len (queries)} searches" )

 1340 +

 1341 + # Claude extraction in batches

 1342 + client = anthropic.Anthropic( api_key = api_key, timeout = 90.0 )

 1343 + items_with_results = [(i, no_state[i], search_results.get(i, "" ))

 1344 + for i in range ( len (no_state)) if search_results.get(i)]

 1345 +

 1346 + batch_size = 30

 1347 + resolved = 0

 1348 + for batch_start in range ( 0 , len (items_with_results), batch_size):

 1349 + batch = items_with_results[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]

 1350 +

 1351 + prompt_lines = []

 1352 + for idx, (i, lead, content) in enumerate (batch):

 1353 + prompt_lines.append(

 1354 + f " {idx + 1 } . email: {lead[ 'Email' ]} | company: {lead.get( 'Company Name' , '' )} | "

 1355 + f "name: {lead.get( 'First Name' , '' )} {lead.get( 'Last Name' , '' )} \n "

 1356 + f " Search results: {content[: 500 ]} "

 1357 + )

 1358 +

 1359 + prompt = f """Determine the US state for each lead based on search results.

 1360 + Many are educators from a California CUE conference.

 1361 +

 1362 + Return JSON array: "num" (1-indexed), "state" (2-letter US state or "" if truly unknown/international),

 1363 + "county" (lowercase, for CA), "org_name" (organization name if identifiable), "org_type" ("school"/"district"/""),

 1364 + "district_name", "is_intl" (true only if clearly not US).

 1365 +

 1366 + ONLY the JSON array.

 1367 +

 1368 + { chr ( 10 ).join(prompt_lines)} """

 1369 +

 1370 + try :

 1371 + response = client.messages.create(

 1372 + model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" ,

 1373 + max_tokens = 4000 ,

 1374 + messages = [{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 1375 + )

 1376 + text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 1377 + bs = text.find( "[" )

 1378 + be = text.rfind( "]" )

 1379 + if bs >= 0 and be > bs:

 1380 + text = text[bs:be + 1 ]

 1381 + results = json.loads(text)

 1382 +

 1383 + for result in results:

 1384 + num = result.get( "num" , 0 ) - 1

 1385 + if 0 <= num < len (batch):

 1386 + lead = batch[num][ 1 ]

 1387 + if result.get( "is_intl" ):

 1388 + lead[ "Country" ] = "INTL"

 1389 + resolved += 1

 1390 + continue

 1391 + state = result.get( "state" , "" )

 1392 + if state and state.lower() != "unknown" and len (state) == 2 :

 1393 + lead[ "State" ] = state.upper()

 1394 + resolved += 1

 1395 + org_name = result.get( "org_name" , "" )

 1396 + if org_name and not lead[ "Company Name" ]:

 1397 + lead[ "Company Name" ] = org_name

 1398 + if result.get( "org_type" ) and not lead[ "Org Type" ]:

 1399 + lead[ "Org Type" ] = result[ "org_type" ]

 1400 + if result.get( "district_name" ) and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1401 + lead[ "District Name" ] = result[ "district_name" ]

 1402 + county = result.get( "county" , "" )

 1403 + if county and lead.get( "State" ) == "CA" and not lead[ "County" ]:

 1404 + lead[ "County" ] = county.title()

 1405 + lead[ "Nor Cal/So Cal" ] = norcal_or_socal(county)

 1406 + except Exception as e:

 1407 + logger.error( f " Final resolve Claude error: {e} " )

 1408 +

 1409 + logger.info( f " Final pass resolved: {resolved} leads" )

 1410 +

 1411 +

 1412 + def _resolve_norcal_socal ( ca_leads : list [ dict ]):

 1413 + """Resolve Nor Cal/So Cal for CA leads missing county info using Serper + Claude."""

 1414 + import anthropic

 1415 + from concurrent.futures import Thread Pool Executor, as_completed

 1416 + import httpx

 1417 +

 1418 + serper_key = os.environ.get( "SERPER_API_KEY" , "" )

 1419 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1420 + if not serper_key or not api_key:

 1421 + return

 1422 +

 1423 + # First try: fill from known domain→county map

 1424 + filled = 0

 1425 + for lead in ca_leads:

 1426 + if lead.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" ):

 1427 + continue

 1428 + root = extract_domain_root(lead[ "Email" ])

 1429 + county = CA_DOMAIN_TO_COUNTY.get(root, "" )

 1430 + if county:

 1431 + lead[ "County" ] = county.title()

 1432 + lead[ "Nor Cal/So Cal" ] = norcal_or_socal(county)

 1433 + filled += 1

 1434 + if filled:

 1435 + logger.info( f " Filled {filled} from domain→county map" )

 1436 +

 1437 + # Remaining

 1438 + still_unknown = [l for l in ca_leads if not l.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" )]

 1439 + if not still_unknown:

 1440 + return

 1441 +

 1442 + logger.info( f " { len (still_unknown)} still need Nor Cal/So Cal — searching..." )

 1443 +

 1444 + def _serper_search ( query ):

 1445 + try :

 1446 + with httpx.Client( timeout = 10.0 ) as http:

 1447 + resp = http.post(

 1448 + "https://google.serper.dev/search" ,

 1449 + headers = { "X-API-KEY" : serper_key, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 1450 + json = { "q" : query + " California county location" , "num" : 3 },

 1451 + )

 1452 + if resp.status_code != 200 :

 1453 + return ""

 1454 + data = resp.json()

 1455 + parts = []

 1456 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 3 ]:

 1457 + parts.append( f " {item.get( 'title' , '' )} | {item.get( 'snippet' , '' )} | {item.get( 'link' , '' )} " )

 1458 + kg = data.get( "knowledgeGraph" , {})

 1459 + if kg:

 1460 + attrs = kg.get( "attributes" , {})

 1461 + addr = attrs.get( "Address" , "" ) or attrs.get( "Headquarters" , "" )

 1462 + parts.append( f "[KG] {kg.get( 'title' , '' )} | {kg.get( 'description' , '' )} | {addr} " )

 1463 + return " \n " .join(parts) if parts else ""

 1464 + except Exception :

 1465 + return ""

 1466 +

 1467 + # Build queries — use company name or email domain

 1468 + search_results = {}

 1469 + queries = {}

 1470 + for i, lead in enumerate (still_unknown):

 1471 + company = lead.get( "Company Name" , "" )

 1472 + domain = lead[ "Email" ].split( "@" )[ - 1 ].lower()

 1473 + if company:

 1474 + queries[i] = company

 1475 + elif domain not in _GENERIC_DOMAINS :

 1476 + queries[i] = domain

 1477 +

 1478 + with Thread Pool Executor( max_workers = 20 ) as executor:

 1479 + future_map = {executor.submit(_serper_search, q): i for i, q in queries.items()}

 1480 + for future in as_completed(future_map):

 1481 + search_results[future_map[future]] = future.result() or ""

 1482 +

 1483 + # Claude batch for county extraction

 1484 + client = anthropic.Anthropic( api_key = api_key, timeout = 90.0 )

 1485 + items = [(i, still_unknown[i], search_results.get(i, "" ))

 1486 + for i in range ( len (still_unknown)) if search_results.get(i)]

 1487 +

 1488 + batch_size = 40

 1489 + resolved = 0

 1490 + for batch_start in range ( 0 , len (items), batch_size):

 1491 + batch = items[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]

 1492 + prompt_lines = []

 1493 + for idx, (i, lead, content) in enumerate (batch):

 1494 + prompt_lines.append(

 1495 + f " {idx + 1 } . company: {lead.get( 'Company Name' , '' )} | email domain: {lead[ 'Email' ].split( '@' )[ - 1 ]} \n "

 1496 + f " Search: {content[: 400 ]} "

 1497 + )

 1498 +

 1499 + prompt = f """These are California education organizations. Determine the COUNTY for each.

 1500 +

 1501 + Return JSON array: "num" (1-indexed), "county" (lowercase California county name).

 1502 +

 1503 + { chr ( 10 ).join(prompt_lines)} """

 1504 +

 1505 + try :

 1506 + response = client.messages.create(

 1507 + model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" ,

 1508 + max_tokens = 2000 ,

 1509 + messages = [{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 1510 + )

 1511 + text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 1512 + bs = text.find( "[" )

 1513 + be = text.rfind( "]" )

 1514 + if bs >= 0 and be > bs:

 1515 + text = text[bs:be + 1 ]

 1516 + results = json.loads(text)

 1517 + for result in results:

 1518 + num = result.get( "num" , 0 ) - 1

 1519 + if 0 <= num < len (batch):

 1520 + lead = batch[num][ 1 ]

 1521 + county = result.get( "county" , "" )

 1522 + if county and county.lower() != "unknown" :

 1523 + lead[ "County" ] = county.title()

 1524 + lead[ "Nor Cal/So Cal" ] = norcal_or_socal(county)

 1525 + if lead[ "Nor Cal/So Cal" ]:

 1526 + resolved += 1

 1527 + except Exception as e:

 1528 + logger.error( f " Nor Cal/So Cal Claude error: {e} " )

 1529 +

 1530 + logger.info( f " Nor Cal/So Cal resolved: {resolved} leads" )

 1531 +

 1532 +

 1533 def _build_domain_state_lookup () -> dict :

 1260 + def _fill_missing_columns ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 1261 + """Fill School Name, District Name, Org Type from data we already have.

 1262 +

 1263 + For every lead:

 1264 + - If Org Type=school and School Name empty → copy from Company Name

 1265 + - If no Org Type but has Company Name → classify it

 1266 + - If Org Type=school and District Name empty → look up in NCES, or use Claude

 1267 + """

 1268 + import anthropic

 1269 +

 1270 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1271 +

 1272 + # Load NCES school→district mapping

 1273 + _ , nces_school_to_district, _ = _load_nces_names()

 1274 +

 1275 + def _normalize_key ( name ):

 1276 + return re.sub( r ' [ ^ a-z0-9] ' , '' , name.lower())

 1277 +

 1278 + filled_school = 0

 1279 + filled_org = 0

 1280 +

 1281 + for lead in leads:

 1282 + company = lead.get( "Company Name" , "" )

 1283 + if not company:

 1284 + continue

 1285 +

 1286 + # Classify if missing

 1287 + if not lead[ "Org Type" ]:

 1288 + org_type, school_name, district_name = classify_org(company)

 1289 + if org_type:

 1290 + lead[ "Org Type" ] = org_type

 1291 + filled_org += 1

 1292 + if school_name and not lead[ "School Name" ]:

 1293 + lead[ "School Name" ] = school_name

 1294 + if district_name and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1295 + lead[ "District Name" ] = district_name

 1296 +

 1297 + # If school but no School Name → copy from Company

 1298 + if lead[ "Org Type" ] == "school" and not lead[ "School Name" ]:

 1299 + lead[ "School Name" ] = company

 1300 + filled_school += 1

 1301 +

 1302 + # If school but no District Name → NCES lookup

 1303 + if lead[ "Org Type" ] == "school" and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1304 + key = _normalize_key(company)

 1305 + if key in nces_school_to_district:

 1306 + lead[ "District Name" ] = nces_school_to_district[key]

 1307 +

 1308 + logger.info( f " Filled {filled_school} School Names, classified {filled_org} Org Types from Company Name" )

 1309 +

 1310 + # For remaining schools without district, use Claude with the info we have

 1311 + still_no_district = [l for l in leads

 1312 + if l.get( "Org Type" ) == "school" and not l.get( "District Name" )

 1313 + and l.get( "Company Name" )]

 1314 + if still_no_district and api_key:

 1315 + logger.info( f " Looking up parent district for { len (still_no_district)} schools via Claude..." )

 1316 + client = anthropic.Anthropic( api_key = api_key, timeout = 90.0 )

 1317 +

 1318 + batch_size = 40

 1319 + resolved = 0

 1320 + for batch_start in range ( 0 , len (still_no_district), batch_size):

 1321 + batch = still_no_district[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]

 1322 + prompt_lines = []

 1323 + for idx, lead in enumerate (batch):

 1324 + state = lead.get( "State" , "" )

 1325 + county = lead.get( "County" , "" )

 1326 + prompt_lines.append(

 1327 + f " {idx + 1 } . school: {lead[ 'Company Name' ]} | state: {state} | county: {county} "

 1328 + )

 1329 +

 1330 + prompt = f """For each school, identify its parent school district.

 1331 +

 1332 + Return JSON array: "num" (1-indexed), "district_name" (full official district name).

 1333 +

 2156 + def route_leads ( leads : list [ dict ]) -> dict :

 2157 + """Route leads to rep tabs. Returns dict of rep_name → list of leads."""

 2158 + steven, tom, liz, shan, cue_calie = [], [], [], [], []

 2159 + unknown_pool = [] # for 50/50 split

 2160 + americas_pool = [] # for round-robin 3-way split

 2161 +

 2162 + for lead in leads:

 2163 + # CUE/CALIE excluded from all rep tabs

 2164 + if lead.get( "CUE/CALIE Flag" ):

 2165 + cue_calie.append(lead)

 2166 + continue

 2167 +

 2168 + country = lead.get( "Country" , "US" )

 2169 + state = lead.get( "State" , "" )

 2170 + region = lead.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "" )

 2171 +

 2172 + # Non-Americas international → Shan

 2173 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) == "Portugal" :

 2174 + shan.append(lead)

 2175 + continue

 2176 +

 2177 + # Americas international → pool for 3-way split

 2178 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) in ( "1" , "2" , "3" ):

 2179 + americas_pool.append(lead)

 2180 + continue

 2181 +

 2182 + # US with no state → pool for 50/50 Steven/Tom

 2183 + if not state:

 2184 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2185 + continue

 2186 +

 2187 + # CA → split by Nor Cal/So Cal

 2188 + if state == "CA" :

 2189 + if region == "SoCal" :

 2190 + steven.append(lead)

 2191 + elif region == "NorCal" :

 2192 + tom.append(lead)

 2193 + else :

 2194 + # Unknown region — split 50/50

 2195 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2196 + continue

 2197 +

 2198 + # Other US states

 2199 + if state in STEVEN_STATES :

 2200 + steven.append(lead)

 2201 + elif state in TOM_STATES :

 2202 + tom.append(lead)

 2203 + elif state in LIZ_STATES :

 2204 + liz.append(lead)

 2205 + else :

 2206 + # Unlisted state — goes to Steven as catch-all

 2207 + steven.append(lead)

 2208 +

 2209 + # Split unknown pool 50/50 between Steven and Tom

 2210 + for i, lead in enumerate (unknown_pool):

 2211 + if i % 2 == 0 :

 2212 + steven.append(lead)

 2213 + else :

 2214 + tom.append(lead)

 2215 +

 2216 + # Split Americas international round-robin between Steven, Tom, Liz

 2217 + for i, lead in enumerate (americas_pool):

 2218 + if i % 3 == 0 :

 2219 + steven.append(lead)

 2220 + elif i % 3 == 1 :

 2221 + tom.append(lead)

 2222 + else :

 2223 + liz.append(lead)

 2224 +

 2225 + return {

 2226 + "Steven" : steven,

 2227 + "Tom" : tom,

 2228 + "Liz" : liz,

 2229 + "Shan" : shan,

 2230 + "CUE-CALIE" : cue_calie,

 2231 + }

 2232 +

 2233 +

 2234 + def write_rep_tabs ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 2235 + """Route leads and write each rep's tab to Google Sheet."""

 2236 + from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials

 2237 + from googleapiclient.discovery import build

 2238 +

 2239 + creds_json = os.environ.get( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON" )

 2240 + if not creds_json:

 2241 + logger.error( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON not set" )

 2242 + return

 2243 +

 2244 + creds = Credentials.from_service_account_info(

 2245 + json.loads(creds_json),

 2246 + scopes = [ "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets" ],

 2247 + )

 2248 + service = build( "sheets" , "v4" , credentials = creds)

 2249 +

 2250 + routed = route_leads(leads)

 2251 +

 2252 + headers = [

 2253 + "First Name" , "Last Name" , "Email" , "Company Name" , "Title" ,

 2254 + "State" , "Country" , "Org Type" , "School Name" , "District Name" ,

 2255 + "County" , "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "CUE/CALIE Flag" ,

 2256 + ]

 2257 +

 2258 + def sort_key ( l ):

 2259 + return (

 2260 + l.get( "State" , "ZZZ" ),

 2261 + { "So Cal" : 0 , "Nor Cal" : 1 }.get(l.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "" ), 2 ),

 2262 + l.get( "District Name" , "" ),

 2263 + l.get( "Last Name" , "" ),

 2264 + )

 2265 +

 2266 + # Get existing tabs

 2267 + meta = service.spreadsheets().get( spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ).execute()

 2268 + existing = {s[ "properties" ][ "title" ]: s[ "properties" ][ "sheetId" ]

 2269 + for s in meta.get( "sheets" , [])}

 2270 +

 2271 + for rep_name, rep_leads in routed.items():

 2272 + tab_name = rep_name

 2273 + rep_leads.sort( key = sort_key)

 2274 +

 2275 + # Create tab if needed

 2276 + if tab_name not in existing:

 2277 + resp = service.spreadsheets().batchUpdate(

 2278 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2279 + body = { "requests" : [{ "addSheet" : { "properties" : { "title" : tab_name}}}]}

 2280 + ).execute()

 2281 + tab_id = resp[ "replies" ][ 0 ][ "add Sheet" ][ "properties" ][ "sheet Id" ]

 2282 + existing[tab_name] = tab_id

 2283 + logger.info( f " Created tab: {tab_name} " )

 2284 + else :

 2285 + tab_id = existing[tab_name]

 2286 + service.spreadsheets().values().clear(

 2287 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2288 + range = f "' {tab_name} '!A:Z" ,

 2289 + ).execute()

 2290 +

 2291 + # Write data

 2292 + rows = [headers]

 2293 + for lead in rep_leads:

 2294 + rows.append([lead.get(h, "" ) for h in headers])

 2295 +

 2296 + service.spreadsheets().values().update(

 2297 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2298 + range = f "' {tab_name} '!A1" ,

 2299 + value Input Option = "RAW" ,

 2300 + body = { "values" : rows},

 2301 + ).execute()

 2302 +

 2303 + logger.info( f " {tab_name} : { len (rep_leads)} leads" )

 2304 +

 2305 + # Highlight CUE/CALIE rows in red on the CUE-CALIE tab

 2306 + if rep_name == "CUE-CALIE" and rep_leads:

 2307 + requests = [{

 2308 + "repeatCell" : {

 2309 + "range" : {

 2310 + "sheetId" : tab_id,

 2311 + "start Row Index" : 1 ,

 2312 + "end Row Index" : len (rep_leads) + 1 ,

 2313 + "start Column Index" : 0 ,

 2314 + "end Column Index" : len (headers),

 2315 + },

 2316 + "cell" : {

 2317 + "user Entered Format" : {

 2318 + "backgroundColor" : {

 2319 + "red" : 1.0 ,

 2320 + "green" : 0.8 ,

 2321 + "blue" : 0.8 ,

 2322 + }

 2323 + }

 2324 + },

 2325 + "fields" : "user Entered Format.background Color" ,

 2326 + }

 2327 + }]

 2328 + service.spreadsheets().batchUpdate(

 2329 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2330 + body = { "requests" : requests},

 2331 + ).execute()

 2332 +

 2333 + # Freeze header row

 2334 + service.spreadsheets().batchUpdate(

 2335 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2336 + body = { "requests" : [{

 2337 + "update Sheet Properties" : {

 2338 + "properties" : {

 2339 + "sheetId" : tab_id,

 2340 + "grid Properties" : { "frozen Row Count" : 1 },

 2341 + },

 2342 + "fields" : "grid Properties.frozen Row Count" ,

 2343 + }

 2344 + }]},

 2345 + ).execute()

 2346 +

 2347 + logger.info( f " \n Rep tabs written to: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/ { SHEET_ID } " )

 2348 +

 2349 +

 2350 def main ():

 2351 logger.info( "Loading and cleaning CUE leads..." )

 2352 leads = load_and_clean( str ( CSV_PATH ))

 Are we able to do that now?

 I want to create email sequences for the leads that came by our codecombat booth at the CUE confernce this year (i already have some of these made in my
 claude.ai chat) and also some email sequences for the enriched batch of opt-in CUE leads that we just worked on. It takes forever for me to manually
 copy/paste them in from a google doc or from the claude.ai chat so I want to see if you and scout are able to do that. To be clear, i already know you can
 help me create the copy and subject lines but what i am asking is if you can create the sequences in Outreach.io without me having to copy/paste it in and
 set up each step manually.

 ⏺ Right now, no — we set a hard rule in Session 34 that the Outreach API is read-only only . That was your explicit instruction, and it's in CLAUDE.md as a key
 decision:

 ▎ "Outreach API: read-only ONLY — Steven's explicit instruction. Never add write operations."

 But the Outreach API absolutely supports creating sequences and sequence steps programmatically. If you want to lift that restriction, here's what we'd
 need:

 1. Re-authorize with write scopes — our current OAuth tokens are read-only. We'd need to update the scopes and re-authenticate to get write access.

 2. Build the sequence creation flow — API calls to create a sequence, add steps (emails with subject/body), set timing between steps, etc.

 3. Safety rails — since this writes to a production system your team uses, we'd want confirmation prompts before creating anything.

 The payoff would be big — you could go from "Claude drafts copy → Google Doc → manually copy/paste each step into Outreach" to "Claude drafts copy → Scout
 creates the full sequence in Outreach automatically."

 Want to lift the read-only restriction and build this? Or do you want to think about it first?

 ❯ I have to spot check unfortunately.

 I see emails with this domain @monet.k12.ca.us that should be "Modesto City Schools" why is it labeled Davis?

 I see palmdalesd.org emails that should be Palmdale School District. Why is ist labed Dale?

 I see CUSD 300 that should likely be unabbreviated and normalized to whatever it would be in NCES or the master list data, something like "Community Unit

 School District 300". Or am I worong here? Should it just be CUSD 300?

 I see wwisdom@egusd.net that should be "Elk Grove Unified School District" Why did it get normalized with emails from different domains? The others

 @richgrove.org should be at "Richgrove School District".

 I see some that say Kern High but it sholud be "Kern High School District"

 I see @slane.k12.or.us listed as Lane but it should be "South Lane School District"

 I see Mdusd but that should be "Mt. Diablo Unified School District"

 I see nlmusd but it should be "Norwalk-La Mirada Unified School District"

 I see @nvside.org but it should be "Nevada Society of Innovators and Digital Educators"

 I see pcoe but it should be "Placer County Office of Education (PCOE)"

 I see emails with "@pusd.us" and they are marked as Pomona but should be "Pasadena Unified School District"

 I see emails with "salinascityesd.org" and its labeled "Salina" but it should be "Salinas City Elementary School District"

 I see Sdusd but it should be "San Diego Unified School District"

 I see Sjusd but it should be San Jose Unified School District

 I see @srvusd.net but it should be "San Ramon Valley Unified School District"

 I see "@bhuhsd.k12.ca.us" that should be "Bret Harte Union High School District" but its listed as union with a bunch of other differnt emails.

 I see @auesd.org and its marked as union but should be Armona Union Elementary School District.

 I see @janesvilleschool.org that should be "Janesville Elementary School"

 I see @mvla.net but it should be Mountain View Los Altos High School District

 There are some HUGE mistakes in here (such as all the ones marked pomona)

 ⏺ You're right, these are serious bugs. The root causes:

 1. Aggressive substring matching — my _find_nces_district does if key in nk or nk in key which matches "monet" to something containing "one", "palmdalesd"

 to "dale", etc. This is terrible.

 2. Domain root collision — pusd.org (Pomona) and pusd.us (Pasadena) both have root "pusd", so they get the same mapping.

 3. Short matches — "bhuhsd" matching "union", "auesd" matching "union" from NCES substring.

 The fix: kill the aggressive substring matching entirely, use full domain (not just root) for the mapping, and add the missing districts.

 1. Gmail/personal email leads with abbreviations (Avuhsd, CCSD, EVSC, Lacoe, Mdusd, PCOE, RCOE, Sdusd, Sjusd, TUSD) — these had no institutional email, so

 the normalization by domain couldn't fix them. They need to be normalized by matching the company name text.

 1260 + def _resolve_remaining_states ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 1261 + """Final pass: resolve ALL remaining US leads without a state using Serper + Claude."""

 1262 + import anthropic

 1263 + from concurrent.futures import Thread Pool Executor, as_completed

 1264 + import httpx

 1265 +

 1266 + serper_key = os.environ.get( "SERPER_API_KEY" , "" )

 1267 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1268 + if not serper_key or not api_key:

 1269 + logger.warning( " Missing SERPER_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — skipping" )

 1270 + return

 1271 +

 1272 + no_state = [l for l in leads if l.get( "Country" ) == "US" and not l.get( "State" )]

 1302 + # Build search queries for each lead

 1310 +

 1311 + is_generic = domain in _GENERIC_DOMAINS

 1312 +

 1313 + if not is_generic:

 1318 + queries[i] = domain

 1321 + queries[i] = f '" {company} " {name} '

 1325 + elif name:

 1326 + queries[i] = f '" {name} " educator school district'

 1335 + idx = future_map[future]

 1336 + search_results[idx] = future.result() or ""

 1341 + # Claude extraction in batches

 1351 + prompt_lines = []

 1352 + for idx, (i, lead, content) in enumerate (batch):

 1358 +

 1359 + prompt = f """Determine the US state for each lead based on search results.

 1360 + Many are educators from a California CUE conference.

 1361 +

 1362 + Return JSON array: "num" (1-indexed), "state" (2-letter US state or "" if truly unknown/international),

 1363 + "county" (lowercase, for CA), "org_name" (organization name if identifiable), "org_type" ("school"/"district"/""),

 1364 + "district_name", "is_intl" (true only if clearly not US).

 1365 +

 1366 + ONLY the JSON array.

 1367 +

 1368 + { chr ( 10 ).join(prompt_lines)} """

 1369 +

 1370 + try :

 1371 + response = client.messages.create(

 1372 + model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" ,

 1373 + max_tokens = 4000 ,

 1374 + messages = [{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 1375 + )

 1376 + text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 1377 + bs = text.find( "[" )

 1378 + be = text.rfind( "]" )

 1379 + if bs >= 0 and be > bs:

 1380 + text = text[bs:be + 1 ]

 1381 + results = json.loads(text)

 1382 +

 1383 + for result in results:

 1384 + num = result.get( "num" , 0 ) - 1

 1385 + if 0 <= num < len (batch):

 1386 + lead = batch[num][ 1 ]

 1387 + if result.get( "is_intl" ):

 1388 + lead[ "Country" ] = "INTL"

 1389 + resolved += 1

 1390 + continue

 1391 + state = result.get( "state" , "" )

 1392 + if state and state.lower() != "unknown" and len (state) == 2 :

 1393 + lead[ "State" ] = state.upper()

 1394 + resolved += 1

 1395 + org_name = result.get( "org_name" , "" )

 1396 + if org_name and not lead[ "Company Name" ]:

 1397 + lead[ "Company Name" ] = org_name

 1398 + if result.get( "org_type" ) and not lead[ "Org Type" ]:

 1399 + lead[ "Org Type" ] = result[ "org_type" ]

 1400 + if result.get( "district_name" ) and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1401 + lead[ "District Name" ] = result[ "district_name" ]

 1402 + county = result.get( "county" , "" )

 1404 + lead[ "County" ] = county.title()

 1406 + except Exception as e:

 1408 +

 1409 + logger.info( f " Final pass resolved: {resolved} leads" )

 1260 + def _fill_missing_columns ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 1261 + """Fill School Name, District Name, Org Type from data we already have.

 1262 +

 1263 + For every lead:

 1264 + - If Org Type=school and School Name empty → copy from Company Name

 1265 + - If no Org Type but has Company Name → classify it

 1266 + - If Org Type=school and District Name empty → look up in NCES, or use Claude

 1267 + """

 1268 + import anthropic

 1269 +

 1270 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1271 +

 1272 + # Load NCES school→district mapping

 1273 + _ , nces_school_to_district, _ = _load_nces_names()

 1274 +

 1275 + def _normalize_key ( name ):

 1276 + return re.sub( r ' [ ^ a-z0-9] ' , '' , name.lower())

 1277 +

 1278 + filled_school = 0

 1279 + filled_org = 0

 1280 +

 1281 + for lead in leads:

 1282 + company = lead.get( "Company Name" , "" )

 1283 + if not company:

 1284 + continue

 1285 +

 1286 + # Classify if missing

 1287 + if not lead[ "Org Type" ]:

 1288 + org_type, school_name, district_name = classify_org(company)

 1289 + if org_type:

 1290 + lead[ "Org Type" ] = org_type

 1291 + filled_org += 1

 1292 + if school_name and not lead[ "School Name" ]:

 1293 + lead[ "School Name" ] = school_name

 1294 + if district_name and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1295 + lead[ "District Name" ] = district_name

 1296 +

 1297 + # If school but no School Name → copy from Company

 1298 + if lead[ "Org Type" ] == "school" and not lead[ "School Name" ]:

 1299 + lead[ "School Name" ] = company

 1300 + filled_school += 1

 1301 +

 1302 + # If school but no District Name → NCES lookup

 1303 + if lead[ "Org Type" ] == "school" and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1304 + key = _normalize_key(company)

 1305 + if key in nces_school_to_district:

 1306 + lead[ "District Name" ] = nces_school_to_district[key]

 1307 +

 1308 + logger.info( f " Filled {filled_school} School Names, classified {filled_org} Org Types from Company Name" )

 1309 +

 1310 + # For remaining schools without district, use Claude with the info we have

 1311 + still_no_district = [l for l in leads

 1312 + if l.get( "Org Type" ) == "school" and not l.get( "District Name" )

 1313 + and l.get( "Company Name" )]

 1314 + if still_no_district and api_key:

 1315 + logger.info( f " Looking up parent district for { len (still_no_district)} schools via Claude..." )

 1316 + client = anthropic.Anthropic( api_key = api_key, timeout = 90.0 )

 1317 +

 1318 + batch_size = 40

 1319 + resolved = 0

 1320 + for batch_start in range ( 0 , len (still_no_district), batch_size):

 1321 + batch = still_no_district[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]

 1322 + prompt_lines = []

 1323 + for idx, lead in enumerate (batch):

 1324 + state = lead.get( "State" , "" )

 1325 + county = lead.get( "County" , "" )

 1326 + prompt_lines.append(

 1327 + f " {idx + 1 } . school: {lead[ 'Company Name' ]} | state: {state} | county: {county} "

 1328 + )

 1329 +

 1330 + prompt = f """For each school, identify its parent school district.

 1331 +

 1332 + Return JSON array: "num" (1-indexed), "district_name" (full official district name).

 1333 +

 1334 + { chr ( 10 ).join(prompt_lines)} """

 1335 +

 1336 + try :

 1337 + response = client.messages.create(

 1338 + model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" ,

 1339 + max_tokens = 2000 ,

 1340 + messages = [{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 1341 + )

 1342 + text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 1343 + bs = text.find( "[" )

 1344 + be = text.rfind( "]" )

 1345 + if bs >= 0 and be > bs:

 1346 + text = text[bs:be + 1 ]

 1347 + results = json.loads(text)

 1348 + for result in results:

 1349 + num = result.get( "num" , 0 ) - 1

 1350 + if 0 <= num < len (batch):

 1351 + district = result.get( "district_name" , "" )

 1352 + if district and district.lower() != "unknown" :

 1353 + batch[num][ "District Name" ] = district

 1354 + resolved += 1

 1355 + except Exception as e:

 1356 + logger.error( f " District lookup error: {e} " )

 1357 +

 1358 + logger.info( f " Resolved {resolved} parent districts" )

 1359 +

 1360 +

 2156 + def route_leads ( leads : list [ dict ]) -> dict :

 2157 + """Route leads to rep tabs. Returns dict of rep_name → list of leads."""

 2158 + steven, tom, liz, shan, cue_calie = [], [], [], [], []

 2159 + unknown_pool = [] # for 50/50 split

 2160 + americas_pool = [] # for round-robin 3-way split

 2161 +

 2162 + for lead in leads:

 2163 + # CUE/CALIE excluded from all rep tabs

 2164 + if lead.get( "CUE/CALIE Flag" ):

 2165 + cue_calie.append(lead)

 2166 + continue

 2167 +

 2168 + country = lead.get( "Country" , "US" )

 2169 + state = lead.get( "State" , "" )

 2170 + region = lead.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "" )

 2171 +

 2172 + # Non-Americas international → Shan

 2173 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) == "Portugal" :

 2174 + shan.append(lead)

 2175 + continue

 2176 +

 2177 + # Americas international → pool for 3-way split

 2178 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) in ( "1" , "2" , "3" ):

 2179 + americas_pool.append(lead)

 2180 + continue

 2181 +

 2182 + # US with no state → pool for 50/50 Steven/Tom

 2183 + if not state:

 2184 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2185 + continue

 2186 +

 2187 + # CA → split by Nor Cal/So Cal

 2188 + if state == "CA" :

 2189 + if region == "SoCal" :

 2190 + steven.append(lead)

 2191 + elif region == "NorCal" :

 2192 + tom.append(lead)

 2193 + else :

 2194 + # Unknown region — split 50/50

 2195 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2196 + continue

 2197 +

 2198 + # Other US states

 2199 + if state in STEVEN_STATES :

 2200 + steven.append(lead)

 2201 + elif state in TOM_STATES :

 2202 + tom.append(lead)

 2203 + elif state in LIZ_STATES :

 2204 + liz.append(lead)

 2205 + else :

 2206 + # Unlisted state — goes to Steven as catch-all

 2207 + steven.append(lead)

 2208 +

 2209 + # Split unknown pool 50/50 between Steven and Tom

 2210 + for i, lead in enumerate (unknown_pool):

 2211 + if i % 2 == 0 :

 2212 + steven.append(lead)

 2213 + else :

 2214 + tom.append(lead)

 2215 +

 2216 + # Split Americas international round-robin between Steven, Tom, Liz

 2217 + for i, lead in enumerate (americas_pool):

 2218 + if i % 3 == 0 :

 2219 + steven.append(lead)

 2220 + elif i % 3 == 1 :

 2221 + tom.append(lead)

 2222 + else :

 2223 + liz.append(lead)

 2224 +

 2225 + return {

 2226 + "Steven" : steven,

 2227 + "Tom" : tom,

 2228 + "Liz" : liz,

 2229 + "Shan" : shan,

 2230 + "CUE-CALIE" : cue_calie,

 2231 + }

 2232 +

 2233 +

 2234 + def write_rep_tabs ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 2235 + """Route leads and write each rep's tab to Google Sheet."""

 2236 + from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials

 2237 + from googleapiclient.discovery import build

 2238 +

 2239 + creds_json = os.environ.get( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON" )

 2240 + if not creds_json:

 2241 + logger.error( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON not set" )

 2242 + return

 2243 +

 2244 + creds = Credentials.from_service_account_info(

 2245 + json.loads(creds_json),

 2246 + scopes = [ "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets" ],

 2247 + )

 2248 + service = build( "sheets" , "v4" , credentials = creds)

 2249 +

 2250 + routed = route_leads(leads)

 2251 +

 2252 + headers = [

 2253 + "First Name" , "Last Name" , "Email" , "Company Name" , "Title" ,

 2254 + "State" , "Country" , "Org Type" , "School Name" , "District Name" ,

 2255 + "County" , "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "CUE/CALIE Flag" ,

 2256 + ]

 2257 +

 2258 + def sort_key ( l ):

 2259 + return (

 2260 + l.get( "State" , "ZZZ" ),

 2261 + { "So Cal" : 0 , "Nor Cal" : 1 }.get(l.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "" ), 2 ),

 2262 + l.get( "District Name" , "" ),

 2263 + l.get( "Last Name" , "" ),

 2264 + )

 2265 +

 2266 + # Get existing tabs

 2267 + meta = service.spreadsheets().get( spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ).execute()

 2268 + existing = {s[ "properties" ][ "title" ]: s[ "properties" ][ "sheetId" ]

 2269 + for s in meta.get( "sheets" , [])}

 2270 +

 2271 + for rep_name, rep_leads in routed.items():

 2272 + tab_name = rep_name

 2273 + rep_leads.sort( key = sort_key)

 2274 +

 2275 + # Create tab if needed

 2276 + if tab_name not in existing:

 2277 + resp = service.spreadsheets().batchUpdate(

 2278 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2279 + body = { "requests" : [{ "addSheet" : { "properties" : { "title" : tab_name}}}]}

 2280 + ).execute()

 2281 + tab_id = resp[ "replies" ][ 0 ][ "add Sheet" ][ "properties" ][ "sheet Id" ]

 2282 + existing[tab_name] = tab_id

 2283 + logger.info( f " Created tab: {tab_name} " )

 2284 + else :

 2285 + tab_id = existing[tab_name]

 2286 + service.spreadsheets().values().clear(

 2287 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2288 + range = f "' {tab_name} '!A:Z" ,

 2289 + ).execute()

 2290 +

 2291 + # Write data

 2292 + rows = [headers]

 2293 + for lead in rep_leads:

 2294 + rows.append([lead.get(h, "" ) for h in headers])

 2295 +

 2296 + service.spreadsheets().values().update(

 2297 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2298 + range = f "' {tab_name} '!A1" ,

 2299 + value Input Option = "RAW" ,

 2300 + body = { "values" : rows},

 2301 + ).execute()

 2302 +

 2303 + logger.info( f " {tab_name} : { len (rep_leads)} leads" )

 2304 +

 2305 + # Highlight CUE/CALIE rows in red on the CUE-CALIE tab

 2306 + if rep_name == "CUE-CALIE" and rep_leads:

 2307 + requests = [{

 2308 + "repeatCell" : {

 2309 + "range" : {

 2310 + "sheetId" : tab_id,

 2311 + "start Row Index" : 1 ,

 2312 + "end Row Index" : len (rep_leads) + 1 ,

 2313 + "start Column Index" : 0 ,

 2314 + "end Column Index" : len (headers),

 2315 + },

 2316 + "cell" : {

 2317 + "user Entered Format" : {

 2318 + "backgroundColor" : {

 2319 + "red" : 1.0 ,

 2320 + "green" : 0.8 ,

 2321 + "blue" : 0.8 ,

 2322 + }

 2323 + }

 2324 + },

 2325 + "fields" : "user Entered Format.background Color" ,

 2326 + }

 2327 + }]

 2328 + service.spreadsheets().batchUpdate(

 2329 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2330 + body = { "requests" : requests},

 2331 + ).execute()

 2332 +

 2333 + # Freeze header row

 2335 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2336 + body = { "requests" : [{

 2337 + "update Sheet Properties" : {

 2338 + "properties" : {

 2339 + "sheetId" : tab_id,

 2340 + "grid Properties" : { "frozen Row Count" : 1 },

 2341 + },

 2342 + "fields" : "grid Properties.frozen Row Count" ,

 2343 + }

 2344 + }]},

 2345 + ).execute()

 2346 +

 2347 + logger.info( f " \n Rep tabs written to: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/ { SHEET_ID } " )

 ⏺ Right now, no — we set a hard rule in Session 34 that the Outreach API is read-only only . That was your explicit instruction, and it's in CLAUDE.md as a key

 decision:

 ▎ "Outreach API: read-only ONLY — Steven's explicit instruction. Never add write operations."

 But the Outreach API absolutely supports creating sequences and sequence steps programmatically. If you want to lift that restriction, here's what we'd

 need:

 1. Re-authorize with write scopes — our current OAuth tokens are read-only. We'd need to update the scopes and re-authenticate to get write access.

 2. Build the sequence creation flow — API calls to create a sequence, add steps (emails with subject/body), set timing between steps, etc.

 3. Safety rails — since this writes to a production system your team uses, we'd want confirmation prompts before creating anything.

 The payoff would be big — you could go from "Claude drafts copy → Google Doc → manually copy/paste each step into Outreach" to "Claude drafts copy → Scout

 creates the full sequence in Outreach automatically."

 ⏺ Good news — the Outreach API fully supports creating sequences programmatically. Here's what we need:
 The flow (4 API calls per sequence):

 1. Create the sequence (name, description)

 2. Create sequence steps (timing between emails, auto vs manual send)

 1. Create the sequence (name, description)
 2 sequence st ps (timing between emails, auto vs manual send)
 3 email t mplates (subject + HTML body)
 4. Link templates to steps

 What needs to happen first:

 Your current Outreach OAuth app is read-only. We need to add write scopes and re-authorize. This means:

 1. You go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api and edit the Scout app registration

 2. Add these scopes: sequences.all , sequence Steps.all , templates.all , sequence Templates.all

 3. Optionally sequenceStates.all if you want Scout to also add prospects to sequences

 4. Re-authorize through the OAuth flow (the existing refresh token won't gain new scopes automatically)

 One question before you do that: Do you want sequences created as auto_email (sends automatically on the interval) or manual_email (creates a task in
 Outreach for you to review and send each one)? Given your "all sequences are drafts" rule, manual_email might be the safer starting point.

 Want to go update the OAuth scopes now? Once you re-authorize, I can build the sequence creation feature.

 ⏺ Here's exactly what to do, step by step:
 Step 1: Update the OAuth app scopes

 1. Go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api

 2. Find the Scout app (the one we set up in Session 34)

 3. Click edit/settings on it
 4. Find the scopes/permissions section

 5. Add these scopes (keep all existing read scopes too):
 - sequences.all
 - sequenceSteps.all
 - templates.all

 - sequenceTemplates.all
 - sequenceStates.all

 - mailboxes.read (may already be there)
 6. Save the app

 Step 2: Re-authorize

 After saving the updated scopes, you need to re-authorize so we get a new token with write permissions. Let me generate the authorization URL for you.

 import sys, json

 d = json.load(sys.stdin)

 client_id = d.get('OUTREACH_CLIENT_ID', '')

 print(f'Client ID: {client_id}')

 print(f'Redirect URI: {redirect_uri}')

 scopes = 'sequences.all+sequence Steps.all+templates.all+sequence Templates.all+sequence States.all+mailboxes.read+prospects.read+sequence Steps.read+sequen

 ces.read+mailings.read+users.read'

 print(f'')

 print(f'Auth URL:')

 print(f'https://api.outreach.io/oauth/authorize?client_id={client_id}&redirect_uri={redirect_uri}&response_type=code&scope={scopes}')

 ⏺ Your "Claude Token for Scout" Git Hub fine-grained personal access token has expired. This is the token Scout uses to persist memory to Git Hub ( GITHUB_TOKEN
 on Railway). If it's expired, Scout can't commit memory updates.

 You need to regenerate it:

 1. Click "Regenerate your token" in that email (or go to https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/11872540/regenerate )

 2. Set a new expiration (I'd suggest 1 year this time so we don't hit this again soon)

 3. Make sure it still has contents:write permission on the scadkin/firstcocoagent repo
 4. Copy the new token

 ⏺ Update ( scripts/enrich_cue_leads.py )

 ⎿   Added 152 lines

 1257 return leads

 1260 + def _resolve_remaining_states ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 1261 + """Final pass: resolve ALL remaining US leads without a state using Serper + Claude."""

 1262 + import anthropic

 1263 + from concurrent.futures import Thread Pool Executor, as_completed

 1264 + import httpx

 1265 +

 1266 + serper_key = os.environ.get( "SERPER_API_KEY" , "" )

 1267 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1268 + if not serper_key or not api_key:

 1269 + logger.warning( " Missing SERPER_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — skipping" )

 1270 + return

 1271 +

 1272 + no_state = [l for l in leads if l.get( "Country" ) == "US" and not l.get( "State" )]

 1273 + if not no_state:

 1274 + logger.info( " No remaining US leads without state" )

 1275 + return

 1276 +

 1277 + logger.info( f " { len (no_state)} US leads still without state" )

 1278 +

 1279 + def _serper_search ( query ):

 1280 + try :

 1281 + with httpx.Client( timeout = 10.0 ) as http:

 1282 + resp = http.post(

 1283 + "https://google.serper.dev/search" ,

 1284 + headers = { "X-API-KEY" : serper_key, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 1285 + json = { "q" : query, "num" : 3 },

 1286 + )

 1287 + if resp.status_code != 200 :

 1288 + return ""

 1289 + data = resp.json()

 1290 + parts = []

 1291 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 3 ]:

 1292 + parts.append( f " {item.get( 'title' , '' )} | {item.get( 'snippet' , '' )} | {item.get( 'link' , '' )} " )

 1293 + kg = data.get( "knowledgeGraph" , {})

 1294 + if kg:

 1295 + attrs = kg.get( "attributes" , {})

 1296 + addr = attrs.get( "Address" , "" ) or attrs.get( "Headquarters" , "" )

 1297 + parts.append( f "[KG] {kg.get( 'title' , '' )} | {kg.get( 'description' , '' )} | {addr} " )

 1298 + return " \n " .join(parts) if parts else ""

 1299 + except Exception :

 1300 + return ""

 1301 +

 1302 + # Build search queries for each lead

 1303 + queries = {} # lead index → query

 1304 + for i, lead in enumerate (no_state):

 1305 + email = lead[ "Email" ]

 1306 + domain = email.split( "@" )[ - 1 ].lower() if "@" in email else ""

 1307 + company = lead.get( "Company Name" , "" )

 1308 + name = f " {lead.get( 'First Name' , '' )} {lead.get( 'Last Name' , '' )} " .strip()

 1309 + title = lead.get( "Title" , "" )

 1310 +

 1311 + is_generic = domain in _GENERIC_DOMAINS

 1312 +

 1313 + if not is_generic:

 1314 + # Institutional: search domain + company name if available

 1315 + if company:

 1316 + queries[i] = f " {company} {domain} "

 1317 + else :

 1318 + queries[i] = domain

 1319 + elif company:

 1320 + # Generic email + company: search company + name

 1321 + queries[i] = f '" {company} " {name} '

 1322 + elif name and title:

 1323 + # Generic email, no company: search name + title + educator

 1324 + queries[i] = f '" {name} " {title} educator school'

 1325 + elif name:

 1326 + queries[i] = f '" {name} " educator school district'

 1327 +

 1328 + logger.info( f " Searching { len (queries)} leads..." )

 1329 +

 1330 + # Parallel Serper search

 1331 + search_results = {}

 1332 + with Thread Pool Executor( max_workers = 20 ) as executor:

 1333 + future_map = {executor.submit(_serper_search, q): i for i, q in queries.items()}

 1334 + for future in as_completed(future_map):

 1335 + idx = future_map[future]

 1336 + search_results[idx] = future.result() or ""

 1337 +

 1338 + got = sum ( 1 for v in search_results.values() if v)

 1339 + logger.info( f " Got results for {got} / { len (queries)} searches" )

 1340 +

 1341 + # Claude extraction in batches

 1342 + client = anthropic.Anthropic( api_key = api_key, timeout = 90.0 )

 1343 + items_with_results = [(i, no_state[i], search_results.get(i, "" ))

 1344 + for i in range ( len (no_state)) if search_results.get(i)]

 1345 +

 1346 + batch_size = 30

 1347 + resolved = 0

 1348 + for batch_start in range ( 0 , len (items_with_results), batch_size):

 1349 + batch = items_with_results[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]

 1350 +

 1351 + prompt_lines = []

 1352 + for idx, (i, lead, content) in enumerate (batch):

 1353 + prompt_lines.append(

 1354 + f " {idx + 1 } . email: {lead[ 'Email' ]} | company: {lead.get( 'Company Name' , '' )} | "

 1412 + def _resolve_norcal_socal ( ca_leads : list [ dict ]):

 1413 + """Resolve Nor Cal/So Cal for CA leads missing county info using Serper + Claude."""

 1414 + import anthropic

 1415 + from concurrent.futures import Thread Pool Executor, as_completed

 1416 + import httpx

 1417 +

 1418 + serper_key = os.environ.get( "SERPER_API_KEY" , "" )

 1419 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1420 + if not serper_key or not api_key:

 1421 + return

 1422 +

 1423 + # First try: fill from known domain→county map

 1424 + filled = 0

 1425 + for lead in ca_leads:

 1426 + if lead.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" ):

 1427 + continue

 1428 + root = extract_domain_root(lead[ "Email" ])

 1429 + county = CA_DOMAIN_TO_COUNTY.get(root, "" )

 1430 + if county:

 1431 + lead[ "County" ] = county.title()

 1432 + lead[ "Nor Cal/So Cal" ] = norcal_or_socal(county)

 1433 + filled += 1

 1434 + if filled:

 1435 + logger.info( f " Filled {filled} from domain→county map" )

 1436 +

 1437 + # Remaining

 1438 + still_unknown = [l for l in ca_leads if not l.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" )]

 1439 + if not still_unknown:

 1440 + return

 1441 +

 1442 + logger.info( f " { len (still_unknown)} still need Nor Cal/So Cal — searching..." )

 1443 +

 1444 + def _serper_search ( query ):

 1445 + try :

 1446 + with httpx.Client( timeout = 10.0 ) as http:

 1447 + resp = http.post(

 1448 + "https://google.serper.dev/search" ,

 1449 + headers = { "X-API-KEY" : serper_key, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 1450 + json = { "q" : query + " California county location" , "num" : 3 },

 1451 + )

 1452 + if resp.status_code != 200 :

 1453 + return ""

 1454 + data = resp.json()

 1455 + parts = []

 1456 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 3 ]:

 1457 + parts.append( f " {item.get( 'title' , '' )} | {item.get( 'snippet' , '' )} | {item.get( 'link' , '' )} " )

 1458 + kg = data.get( "knowledgeGraph" , {})

 1459 + if kg:

 1460 + attrs = kg.get( "attributes" , {})

 1461 + addr = attrs.get( "Address" , "" ) or attrs.get( "Headquarters" , "" )

 1462 + parts.append( f "[KG] {kg.get( 'title' , '' )} | {kg.get( 'description' , '' )} | {addr} " )

 1463 + return " \n " .join(parts) if parts else ""

 1464 + except Exception :

 1465 + return ""

 1466 +

 1467 + # Build queries — use company name or email domain

 1468 + search_results = {}

 1469 + queries = {}

 1470 + for i, lead in enumerate (still_unknown):

 1471 + company = lead.get( "Company Name" , "" )

 1472 + domain = lead[ "Email" ].split( "@" )[ - 1 ].lower()

 1473 + if company:

 1474 + queries[i] = company

 1475 + elif domain not in _GENERIC_DOMAINS :

 1476 + queries[i] = domain

 1477 +

 1478 + with Thread Pool Executor( max_workers = 20 ) as executor:

 1479 + future_map = {executor.submit(_serper_search, q): i for i, q in queries.items()}

 1480 + for future in as_completed(future_map):

 1481 + search_results[future_map[future]] = future.result() or ""

 1482 +

 1483 + # Claude batch for county extraction

 1484 + client = anthropic.Anthropic( api_key = api_key, timeout = 90.0 )

 1485 + items = [(i, still_unknown[i], search_results.get(i, "" ))

 1486 + for i in range ( len (still_unknown)) if search_results.get(i)]

 1487 +

 1488 + batch_size = 40

 1489 + resolved = 0

 1490 + for batch_start in range ( 0 , len (items), batch_size):

 1491 + batch = items[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]

 1492 + prompt_lines = []

 1493 + for idx, (i, lead, content) in enumerate (batch):

 1494 + prompt_lines.append(

 1495 + f " {idx + 1 } . company: {lead.get( 'Company Name' , '' )} | email domain: {lead[ 'Email' ].split( '@' )[ - 1 ]} \n "

 1496 + f " Search: {content[: 400 ]} "

 1497 + )

 1498 +

 1499 + prompt = f """These are California education organizations. Determine the COUNTY for each.

 1500 +

 1501 + Return JSON array: "num" (1-indexed), "county" (lowercase California county name).

 1502 +

 1503 + { chr ( 10 ).join(prompt_lines)} """

 1504 +

 1505 + try :

 1506 + response = client.messages.create(

 1507 + model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" ,

 1508 + max_tokens = 2000 ,

 1509 + messages = [{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 1260 + def _fill_missing_columns ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 1261 + """Fill School Name, District Name, Org Type from data we already have.

 1262 +

 1263 + For every lead:

 1264 + - If Org Type=school and School Name empty → copy from Company Name

 1265 + - If no Org Type but has Company Name → classify it

 1266 + - If Org Type=school and District Name empty → look up in NCES, or use Claude

 1267 + """

 1268 + import anthropic

 1269 +

 1270 + api_key = os.environ.get( "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" , "" )

 1271 +

 1272 + # Load NCES school→district mapping

 1273 + _ , nces_school_to_district, _ = _load_nces_names()

 1274 +

 1275 + def _normalize_key ( name ):

 1276 + return re.sub( r ' [ ^ a-z0-9] ' , '' , name.lower())

 1277 +

 1278 + filled_school = 0

 1279 + filled_org = 0

 1280 +

 1281 + for lead in leads:

 1282 + company = lead.get( "Company Name" , "" )

 1283 + if not company:

 1284 + continue

 1285 +

 1286 + # Classify if missing

 1287 + if not lead[ "Org Type" ]:

 1288 + org_type, school_name, district_name = classify_org(company)

 1289 + if org_type:

 1290 + lead[ "Org Type" ] = org_type

 1291 + filled_org += 1

 1292 + if school_name and not lead[ "School Name" ]:

 1293 + lead[ "School Name" ] = school_name

 1294 + if district_name and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1295 + lead[ "District Name" ] = district_name

 1296 +

 1297 + # If school but no School Name → copy from Company

 1298 + if lead[ "Org Type" ] == "school" and not lead[ "School Name" ]:

 1299 + lead[ "School Name" ] = company

 1300 + filled_school += 1

 1301 +

 1302 + # If school but no District Name → NCES lookup

 1303 + if lead[ "Org Type" ] == "school" and not lead[ "District Name" ]:

 1304 + key = _normalize_key(company)

 1305 + if key in nces_school_to_district:

 1306 + lead[ "District Name" ] = nces_school_to_district[key]

 1307 +

 1308 + logger.info( f " Filled {filled_school} School Names, classified {filled_org} Org Types from Company Name" )

 1309 +

 1310 + # For remaining schools without district, use Claude with the info we have

 1311 + still_no_district = [l for l in leads

 1312 + if l.get( "Org Type" ) == "school" and not l.get( "District Name" )

 1313 + and l.get( "Company Name" )]

 1314 + if still_no_district and api_key:

 1315 + logger.info( f " Looking up parent district for { len (still_no_district)} schools via Claude..." )

 1316 + client = anthropic.Anthropic( api_key = api_key, timeout = 90.0 )

 1317 +

 1318 + batch_size = 40

 1319 + resolved = 0

 1320 + for batch_start in range ( 0 , len (still_no_district), batch_size):

 1321 + batch = still_no_district[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]

 1322 + prompt_lines = []

 1323 + for idx, lead in enumerate (batch):

 1324 + state = lead.get( "State" , "" )

 1325 + county = lead.get( "County" , "" )

 1326 + prompt_lines.append(

 1327 + f " {idx + 1 } . school: {lead[ 'Company Name' ]} | state: {state} | county: {county} "

 1328 + )

 1329 +

 1330 + prompt = f """For each school, identify its parent school district.

 1331 +

 1332 + Return JSON array: "num" (1-indexed), "district_name" (full official district name).

 1333 +

 1334 + { chr ( 10 ).join(prompt_lines)} """

 1335 +

 1336 + try :

 1337 + response = client.messages.create(

 1338 + model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" ,

 1339 + max_tokens = 2000 ,

 1340 + messages = [{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 1341 + )

 1342 + text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 1343 + bs = text.find( "[" )

 1344 + be = text.rfind( "]" )

 1345 + if bs >= 0 and be > bs:

 1347 + results = json.loads(text)

 1349 + num = result.get( "num" , 0 ) - 1

 2156 + def route_leads ( leads : list [ dict ]) -> dict :

 2157 + """Route leads to rep tabs. Returns dict of rep_name → list of leads."""

 2158 + steven, tom, liz, shan, cue_calie = [], [], [], [], []

 2159 + unknown_pool = [] # for 50/50 split

 2160 + americas_pool = [] # for round-robin 3-way split

 2161 +

 2162 + for lead in leads:

 2163 + # CUE/CALIE excluded from all rep tabs

 2164 + if lead.get( "CUE/CALIE Flag" ):

 2165 + cue_calie.append(lead)

 2166 + continue

 2167 +

 2168 + country = lead.get( "Country" , "US" )

 2169 + state = lead.get( "State" , "" )

 2170 + region = lead.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "" )

 2171 +

 2172 + # Non-Americas international → Shan

 2173 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) == "Portugal" :

 2174 + shan.append(lead)

 2175 + continue

 2176 +

 2177 + # Americas international → pool for 3-way split

 2178 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) in ( "1" , "2" , "3" ):

 2179 + americas_pool.append(lead)

 2180 + continue

 2181 +

 2182 + # US with no state → pool for 50/50 Steven/Tom

 2183 + if not state:

 2184 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2185 + continue

 2186 +

 2187 + # CA → split by Nor Cal/So Cal

 2188 + if state == "CA" :

 2189 + if region == "SoCal" :

 2190 + steven.append(lead)

 2191 + elif region == "NorCal" :

 2192 + tom.append(lead)

 2193 + else :

 2194 + # Unknown region — split 50/50

 2195 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2196 + continue

 2197 +

 2198 + # Other US states

 2199 + if state in STEVEN_STATES :

 2200 + steven.append(lead)

 2201 + elif state in TOM_STATES :

 2202 + tom.append(lead)

 2203 + elif state in LIZ_STATES :

 2204 + liz.append(lead)

 2205 + else :

 2206 + # Unlisted state — goes to Steven as catch-all

 2207 + steven.append(lead)

 2208 +

 2209 + # Split unknown pool 50/50 between Steven and Tom

 2210 + for i, lead in enumerate (unknown_pool):

 2211 + if i % 2 == 0 :

 2212 + steven.append(lead)

 2213 + else :

 2214 + tom.append(lead)

 2215 +

 2216 + # Split Americas international round-robin between Steven, Tom, Liz

 2217 + for i, lead in enumerate (americas_pool):

 2218 + if i % 3 == 0 :

 2219 + steven.append(lead)

 2220 + elif i % 3 == 1 :

 2221 + tom.append(lead)

 2222 + else :

 2223 + liz.append(lead)

 2224 +

 2225 + return {

 2226 + "Steven" : steven,

 2227 + "Tom" : tom,

 2228 + "Liz" : liz,

 2229 + "Shan" : shan,

 2230 + "CUE-CALIE" : cue_calie,

 2231 + }

 2234 + def write_rep_tabs ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 2235 + """Route leads and write each rep's tab to Google Sheet."""

 2237 + from googleapiclient.discovery import build

 2238 +

 2239 + creds_json = os.environ.get( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON" )

 2240 + if not creds_json:

 2242 + return

 2243 +

 2246 + scopes = [ "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets" ],

 2247 + )

 2248 + service = build( "sheets" , "v4" , credentials = creds)

 2249 +

 2250 + routed = route_leads(leads)

 2255 + "County" , "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "CUE/CALIE Flag" ,

 2256 + ]

 2257 +

 2258 + def sort_key ( l ):

 2260 + l.get( "State" , "ZZZ" ),

 2262 + l.get( "District Name" , "" ),

 2263 + l.get( "Last Name" , "" ),

 2264 + )

 2265 +

 2266 + # Get existing tabs

 2267 + meta = service.spreadsheets().get( spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ).execute()

 2268 + existing = {s[ "properties" ][ "title" ]: s[ "properties" ][ "sheetId" ]

 2269 + for s in meta.get( "sheets" , [])}

 2270 +

 ⏺ Agent "Research Outreach API sequences" completed

 ⏺ Good news — the Outreach API fully supports creating sequences programmatically. Here's what we need:

 The flow (4 API calls per sequence):

 1. Create the sequence (name, description)

 2. Create sequence steps (timing between emails, auto vs manual send)

 3. Create email templates (subject + HTML body)

 4. Link templates to steps

 What needs to happen first:

 Your current Outreach OAuth app is read-only. We need to add write scopes and re-authorize. This means:

 1. You go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api and edit the Scout app registration

 2. Add these scopes: sequences.all , sequence Steps.all , templates.all , sequence Templates.all

 3. Optionally sequenceStates.all if you want Scout to also add prospects to sequences

 4. Re-authorize through the OAuth flow (the existing refresh token won't gain new scopes automatically)

 One question before you do that: Do you want sequences created as auto_email (sends automatically on the interval) or manual_email (creates a task in

 Outreach for you to review and send each one)? Given your "all sequences are drafts" rule, manual_email might be the safer starting point.

 Want to go update the OAuth scopes now? Once you re-authorize, I can build the sequence creation feature.

 ❯ I want the sequeneces created as auto_email and the first step should send after 5 minutes. I will reveiew all the sequences before we start adding people

 to the sequences and sending them out. We will need to talk through all the settings to get them optimized eventually.

 Walk me through exaclty what I need to do

 ⏺ Here's exactly what to do, step by step:

 Step 1: Update the OAuth app scopes

 1. Go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api

 2. Find the Scout app (the one we set up in Session 34)

 3. Click edit/settings on it

 4. Find the scopes/permissions section

 5. Add these scopes (keep all existing read scopes too):

 - sequences.all

 - sequenceSteps.all

 - templates.all

 - sequenceTemplates.all

 - sequenceStates.all

 - mailboxes.read (may already be there)

 6. Save the app

 Step 2: Re-authorize

 After saving the updated scopes, you need to re-authorize so we get a new token with write permissions. Let me generate the authorization URL for you.

 Redirect URI: https://web-production-1010b.up.railway.app/oauth/outreach/callback

 ⏺ After you update the scopes in Step 1, do Step 2:

 1. Open this URL in your browser:

 https://api.outreach.io/oauth/authorize?client_id=5L~q8FE3w7yU2~WK.p On2qARfdd8m2f EoDFl~qTM28fa&redirect_uri=https://web-production-1010b.up.railway.app/oaut

 h/outreach/callback&response_type=code&scope=sequences.all+sequence Steps.all+templates.all+sequence Templates.all+sequence States.all+mailboxes.read+prospects

 .read+sequenceSteps.read+sequences.read+mailings.read+users.read
