 2156 + def route_leads ( leads : list [ dict ]) -> dict :

 2157 + """Route leads to rep tabs. Returns dict of rep_name → list of leads."""

 2158 + steven, tom, liz, shan, cue_calie = [], [], [], [], []

 2159 + unknown_pool = [] # for 50/50 split

 2160 + americas_pool = [] # for round-robin 3-way split

 2161 +

 2162 + for lead in leads:

 2163 + # CUE/CALIE excluded from all rep tabs

 2164 + if lead.get( "CUE/CALIE Flag" ):

 2165 + cue_calie.append(lead)

 2166 + continue

 2167 +

 2168 + country = lead.get( "Country" , "US" )

 2169 + state = lead.get( "State" , "" )

 2170 + region = lead.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "" )

 2171 +

 2172 + # Non-Americas international → Shan

 2173 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) == "Portugal" :

 2174 + shan.append(lead)

 2175 + continue

 2176 +

 2177 + # Americas international → pool for 3-way split

 2178 + if country not in ( "US" , "" ) and lead.get( "Round Robin" ) in ( "1" , "2" , "3" ):

 2179 + americas_pool.append(lead)

 2180 + continue

 2181 +

 2182 + # US with no state → pool for 50/50 Steven/Tom

 2183 + if not state:

 2184 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2185 + continue

 2186 +

 2187 + # CA → split by Nor Cal/So Cal

 2188 + if state == "CA" :

 2189 + if region == "SoCal" :

 2190 + steven.append(lead)

 2191 + elif region == "NorCal" :

 2192 + tom.append(lead)

 2193 + else :

 2194 + # Unknown region — split 50/50

 2195 + unknown_pool.append(lead)

 2196 + continue

 2197 +

 2198 + # Other US states

 2199 + if state in STEVEN_STATES :

 2200 + steven.append(lead)

 2201 + elif state in TOM_STATES :

 2202 + tom.append(lead)

 2203 + elif state in LIZ_STATES :

 2204 + liz.append(lead)

 2205 + else :

 2206 + # Unlisted state — goes to Steven as catch-all

 2207 + steven.append(lead)

 2208 +

 2209 + # Split unknown pool 50/50 between Steven and Tom

 2210 + for i, lead in enumerate (unknown_pool):

 2211 + if i % 2 == 0 :

 2212 + steven.append(lead)

 2213 + else :

 2214 + tom.append(lead)

 2215 +

 2216 + # Split Americas international round-robin between Steven, Tom, Liz

 2217 + for i, lead in enumerate (americas_pool):

 2218 + if i % 3 == 0 :

 2219 + steven.append(lead)

 2220 + elif i % 3 == 1 :

 2221 + tom.append(lead)

 2222 + else :

 2223 + liz.append(lead)

 2224 +

 2225 + return {

 2226 + "Steven" : steven,

 2227 + "Tom" : tom,

 2228 + "Liz" : liz,

 2229 + "Shan" : shan,

 2230 + "CUE-CALIE" : cue_calie,

 2231 + }

 2232 +

 2233 +

 2234 + def write_rep_tabs ( leads : list [ dict ]):

 2235 + """Route leads and write each rep's tab to Google Sheet."""

 2236 + from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials

 2237 + from googleapiclient.discovery import build

 2238 +

 2239 + creds_json = os.environ.get( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON" )

 2240 + if not creds_json:

 2241 + logger.error( "GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON not set" )

 2242 + return

 2243 +

 2244 + creds = Credentials.from_service_account_info(

 2245 + json.loads(creds_json),

 2246 + scopes = [ "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets" ],

 2247 + )

 2248 + service = build( "sheets" , "v4" , credentials = creds)

 2249 +

 2250 + routed = route_leads(leads)

 2251 +

 2252 + headers = [

 2253 + "First Name" , "Last Name" , "Email" , "Company Name" , "Title" ,

 2254 + "State" , "Country" , "Org Type" , "School Name" , "District Name" ,

 2255 + "County" , "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "CUE/CALIE Flag" ,

 2256 + ]

 2257 +

 2258 + def sort_key ( l ):

 2259 + return (

 2260 + l.get( "State" , "ZZZ" ),

 2261 + { "So Cal" : 0 , "Nor Cal" : 1 }.get(l.get( "Nor Cal/So Cal" , "" ), 2 ),

 2262 + l.get( "District Name" , "" ),

 2263 + l.get( "Last Name" , "" ),

 2264 + )

 2265 +

 2266 + # Get existing tabs

 2267 + meta = service.spreadsheets().get( spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ).execute()

 2268 + existing = {s[ "properties" ][ "title" ]: s[ "properties" ][ "sheetId" ]

 2269 + for s in meta.get( "sheets" , [])}

 2270 +

 2271 + for rep_name, rep_leads in routed.items():

 2272 + tab_name = rep_name

 2273 + rep_leads.sort( key = sort_key)

 2274 +

 2275 + # Create tab if needed

 2276 + if tab_name not in existing:

 2277 + resp = service.spreadsheets().batchUpdate(

 2278 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2279 + body = { "requests" : [{ "addSheet" : { "properties" : { "title" : tab_name}}}]}

 2280 + ).execute()

 2281 + tab_id = resp[ "replies" ][ 0 ][ "add Sheet" ][ "properties" ][ "sheet Id" ]

 2282 + existing[tab_name] = tab_id

 2283 + logger.info( f " Created tab: {tab_name} " )

 2284 + else :

 2285 + tab_id = existing[tab_name]

 2286 + service.spreadsheets().values().clear(

 2287 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2288 + range = f "' {tab_name} '!A:Z" ,

 2289 + ).execute()

 2290 +

 2291 + # Write data

 2292 + rows = [headers]

 2293 + for lead in rep_leads:

 2294 + rows.append([lead.get(h, "" ) for h in headers])

 2295 +

 2296 + service.spreadsheets().values().update(

 2297 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2298 + range = f "' {tab_name} '!A1" ,

 2299 + value Input Option = "RAW" ,

 2300 + body = { "values" : rows},

 2301 + ).execute()

 2302 +

 2303 + logger.info( f " {tab_name} : { len (rep_leads)} leads" )

 2304 +

 2305 + # Highlight CUE/CALIE rows in red on the CUE-CALIE tab

 2306 + if rep_name == "CUE-CALIE" and rep_leads:

 2307 + requests = [{

 2308 + "repeatCell" : {

 2309 + "range" : {

 2310 + "sheetId" : tab_id,

 2311 + "start Row Index" : 1 ,

 2312 + "end Row Index" : len (rep_leads) + 1 ,

 2313 + "start Column Index" : 0 ,

 2314 + "end Column Index" : len (headers),

 2315 + },

 2316 + "cell" : {

 2317 + "user Entered Format" : {

 2318 + "backgroundColor" : {

 2319 + "red" : 1.0 ,

 2320 + "green" : 0.8 ,

 2321 + "blue" : 0.8 ,

 2322 + }

 2323 + }

 2324 + },

 2325 + "fields" : "user Entered Format.background Color" ,

 2326 + }

 2327 + }]

 2328 + service.spreadsheets().batchUpdate(

 2329 + spreadsheetId = SHEET_ID ,

 2330 + body = { "requests" : requests},

 2331 + ).execute()

 2332 +

 2333 + # Freeze header row

 2336 + body = { "requests" : [{

 2338 + "properties" : {

 2339 + "sheetId" : tab_id,

 2340 + "grid Properties" : { "frozen Row Count" : 1 },

 2341 + },

 2342 + "fields" : "grid Properties.frozen Row Count" ,

 2343 + }

 2344 + }]},

 2345 + ).execute()

 2346 +

 2347 + logger.info( f " \n Rep tabs written to: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/ { SHEET_ID } " )

 ⏺ Right now, no — we set a hard rule in Session 34 that the Outreach API is read-only only . That was your explicit instruction, and it's in CLAUDE.md as a key

 decision:

 ▎ "Outreach API: read-only ONLY — Steven's explicit instruction. Never add write operations."

 But the Outreach API absolutely supports creating sequences and sequence steps programmatically. If you want to lift that restriction, here's what we'd

 need:

 1. Re-authorize with write scopes — our current OAuth tokens are read-only. We'd need to update the scopes and re-authenticate to get write access.

 2. Build the sequence creation flow — API calls to create a sequence, add steps (emails with subject/body), set timing between steps, etc.

 3. Safety rails — since this writes to a production system your team uses, we'd want confirmation prompts before creating anything.

 The payoff would be big — you could go from "Claude drafts copy → Google Doc → manually copy/paste each step into Outreach" to "Claude drafts copy → Scout

 creates the full sequence in Outreach automatically."

 Want to lift the read-only restriction and build this? Or do you want to think about it first?

 ⏺ Agent "Research Outreach API sequences" completed

 ⏺ Good news — the Outreach API fully supports creating sequences programmatically. Here's what we need:

 The flow (4 API calls per sequence):

 1. Create the sequence (name, description)

 2. Create sequence steps (timing between emails, auto vs manual send)

 3. Create email templates (subject + HTML body)

 4. Link templates to steps

 What needs to happen first:

 Your current Outreach OAuth app is read-only. We need to add write scopes and re-authorize. This means:

 1. You go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api and edit the Scout app registration

 2. Add these scopes: sequences.all , sequence Steps.all , templates.all , sequence Templates.all

 3. Optionally sequenceStates.all if you want Scout to also add prospects to sequences

 4. Re-authorize through the OAuth flow (the existing refresh token won't gain new scopes automatically)

 One question before you do that: Do you want sequences created as auto_email (sends automatically on the interval) or manual_email (creates a task in

 Outreach for you to review and send each one)? Given your "all sequences are drafts" rule, manual_email might be the safer starting point.

 Want to go update the OAuth scopes now? Once you re-authorize, I can build the sequence creation feature.

 ❯ I want the sequeneces created as auto_email and the first step should send after 5 minutes. I will reveiew all the sequences before we start adding people

 to the sequences and sending them out. We will need to talk through all the settings to get them optimized eventually.

 Walk me through exaclty what I need to do

 ⏺ Here's exactly what to do, step by step:

 Step 1: Update the OAuth app scopes

 1. Go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api

 2. Find the Scout app (the one we set up in Session 34)

 3. Click edit/settings on it

 4. Find the scopes/permissions section

 5. Add these scopes (keep all existing read scopes too):

 - sequences.all

 - sequenceSteps.all

 - templates.all

 - sequenceTemplates.all

 - sequenceStates.all

 - mailboxes.read (may already be there)

 6. Save the app

 Step 2: Re-authorize

 After saving the updated scopes, you need to re-authorize so we get a new token with write permissions. Let me generate the authorization URL for you.

 ⏺ Your "Claude Token for Scout" Git Hub fine-grained personal access token has expired. This is the token Scout uses to persist memory to Git Hub ( GITHUB_TOKEN

 on Railway). If it's expired, Scout can't commit memory updates.

 3. Make sure it still has contents:write permission on the scadkin/firstcocoagent repo

 337 return resp.json()
 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :
 341 + """
 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).
 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 360 +

 363 """

 364 Paginate through all results for a GET endpoint.

 672 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 673 + # WRITE METHODS — Sequence Creation (Session 38+)

 674 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 675 +

 676 + def get_mailboxes () -> list [ dict ]:

 677 + """Get available mailboxes for sending sequences."""

 678 + raw = _api_get_all( "/mailboxes" )

 679 + mailboxes = []

 680 + for item in raw:

 681 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 682 + mailboxes.append({

 683 + "id" : item.get( "id" ),

 684 + "email" : attrs.get( "email" , "" ),

 685 + "username" : attrs.get( "username" , "" ),

 686 + })

 687 + return mailboxes

 688 +

 689 +

 690 + def create_sequence (

 691 + name : str ,

 692 + steps : list [ dict ],

 693 + description : str = "" ,

 694 + tags : list [ str ] | None = None ,

 695 + ) -> dict :

 696 + """

 697 + Create a complete sequence in Outreach with email steps.

 698 +

 699 + Args:

 700 + name: Sequence name (shown in Outreach UI)

 701 + steps: List of step dicts, each with:

 702 + - subject: Email subject line

 703 + - body_html: HTML email body

 704 + - interval_minutes: Minutes after previous step (first step = 5 min)

 705 + description: Optional sequence description

 706 + tags: Optional list of tags

 707 +

 708 + Returns dict with sequence details + step/template IDs, or error.

 709 +

 710 + Flow:

 711 + 1. Create sequence

 712 + 2. For each step: create template → create sequence Step → link via sequence Template

 713 + """

 714 + logger.info( f "Creating Outreach sequence: {name} ( { len (steps)} steps)" )

 715 +

 716 + # Step 1: Create the sequence

 717 + seq_attrs = {

 718 + "name" : name,

 719 + "sequenceType" : "interval" ,

 720 + }

 721 + if description:

 722 + seq_attrs[ "description" ] = description

 723 + if tags:

 724 + seq_attrs[ "tags" ] = tags

 725 +

 726 + seq_payload = {

 727 + "data" : {

 728 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 729 + "attributes" : seq_attrs,

 730 + }

 731 + }

 732 +

 733 + try :

 734 + seq_result = _api_post( "/sequences" , seq_payload)

 735 + except Exception as e:

 736 + return { "error" : f "Failed to create sequence: {e} " }

 737 +

 738 + seq_id = seq_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 739 + if not seq_id:

 740 + return { "error" : f "No sequence ID returned: {seq_result} " }

 741 +

 742 + logger.info( f " Created sequence ID: {seq_id} " )

 743 +

 744 + created_steps = []

 745 +

 746 + for i, step in enumerate (steps):

 747 + subject = step.get( "subject" , f "Step {i + 1 } " )

 748 + body_html = step.get( "body_html" , "" )

 749 + interval = step.get( "interval_minutes" , 5 if i == 0 else 1440 )

 750 +

 751 + # Step 2a: Create template

 752 + template_payload = {

 753 + "data" : {

 754 + "type" : "template" ,

 755 + "attributes" : {

 756 + "subject" : subject,

 757 + "bodyHtml" : body_html,

 758 + },

 759 + }

 760 + }

 761 +

 762 + try :

 763 + template_result = _api_post( "/templates" , template_payload)

 764 + template_id = template_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 765 + except Exception as e:

 766 + logger.error( f " Failed to create template for step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 767 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "error" : str (e)})

 768 + continue

 769 +

 770 + # Step 2b: Create sequence step

 771 + step_payload = {

 772 + "data" : {

 773 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 774 + "attributes" : {

 775 + "stepType" : "auto_email" ,

 776 + "interval" : interval,

 777 + "order" : i + 1 ,

 778 + },

 779 + "relationships" : {

 780 + "sequence" : {

 781 + "data" : {

 782 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 783 + "id" : int (seq_id),

 784 + }

 785 + }

 786 + },

 787 + }

 788 + }

 789 +

 790 + try :

 791 + step_result = _api_post( "/sequenceSteps" , step_payload)

 792 + step_id = step_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 793 + except Exception as e:

 794 + logger.error( f " Failed to create step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 795 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "template_id" : template_id, "error" : str (e)})

 796 + continue

 797 +

 798 + # Step 2c: Link template to step

 799 + link_payload = {

 800 + "data" : {

 801 + "type" : "sequenceTemplate" ,

 802 + "relationships" : {

 803 + "sequenceStep" : {

 804 + "data" : {

 805 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 806 + "id" : int (step_id),

 807 + }

 808 + },

 809 + "template" : {

 810 + "data" : {

 811 + "type" : "template" ,

 812 + "id" : int (template_id),

 813 + }

 814 + },

 815 + },

 816 + }

 817 + }

 818 +

 819 + try :

 820 + _api_post( "/sequenceTemplates" , link_payload)

 821 + except Exception as e:

 822 + logger.error( f " Failed to link template to step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 823 + created_steps.append({

 824 + "step" : i + 1 , "step_id" : step_id,

 825 + "template_id" : template_id, "error" : f "Link failed: {e} " ,

 826 + })

 827 + continue

 828 +

 829 + created_steps.append({

 830 + "step" : i + 1 ,

 831 + "step_id" : step_id,

 832 + "template_id" : template_id,

 833 + "subject" : subject,

 834 + "interval_minutes" : interval,

 835 + })

 836 + logger.info( f " Step {i + 1 } : template= {template_id} , step= {step_id} , interval= {interval} min, subject= {subject[: 50 ]} " )

 837 +

 838 + result = {

 839 + "sequence_id" : seq_id,

 840 + "name" : name,

 841 + "steps" : created_steps,

 842 + "errors" : [s for s in created_steps if "error" in s],

 843 + }

 844 +

 845 + if result[ "errors" ]:

 846 + logger.warning( f " Sequence created with { len (result[ 'errors' ])} errors" )

 847 + else :

 848 + logger.info( f " Sequence ' {name} ' created successfully with { len (created_steps)} steps" )

 849 +

 850 + return result

 import httpx, json, os

 # Get tokens from GitHub directly

 env_vars = {}

 with open('.env') as f:

 for line in f:

 line = line.strip()

 if not line or line.startswith('#') or '=' not in line: continue

 k, v = line.split('=', 1)

 env_vars[k.strip()] = v.strip()

 token = env_vars.get('GITHUB_TOKEN', '')

 resp = httpx.get(

 'https://api.github.com/repos/scadkin/firstcocoagent/contents/memory/outreach_tokens.json',

 headers={'Authorization': f'token {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github.v3.raw'},

 timeout=15.0,

 )

 print(resp.status_code)

 if resp.status_code == 200:

 data = json.loads(resp.text)

 # Write to local /tmp

 with open('/tmp/outreach_tokens.json', 'w') as f:

 json.dump(data, f)

 print(f'Tokens saved. Has refresh: {bool(data.get(\"refresh_token\"))}')

 ⏺ Right now, no — we set a hard rule in Session 34 that the Outreach API is read-only only . That was your explicit instruction, and it's in CLAUDE.md as a key

 decision:

 ▎ "Outreach API: read-only ONLY — Steven's explicit instruction. Never add write operations."

 But the Outreach API absolutely supports creating sequences and sequence steps programmatically. If you want to lift that restriction, here's what we'd

 need:

 1. Re-authorize with write scopes — our current OAuth tokens are read-only. We'd need to update the scopes and re-authenticate to get write access.

 2. Build the sequence creation flow — API calls to create a sequence, add steps (emails with subject/body), set timing between steps, etc.

 3. Safety rails — since this writes to a production system your team uses, we'd want confirmation prompts before creating anything.

 The payoff would be big — you could go from "Claude drafts copy → Google Doc → manually copy/paste each step into Outreach" to "Claude drafts copy → Scout

 creates the full sequence in Outreach automatically."

 Want to lift the read-only restriction and build this? Or do you want to think about it first?

 ⏺ Agent "Research Outreach API sequences" completed

 ⏺ Good news — the Outreach API fully supports creating sequences programmatically. Here's what we need:

 The flow (4 API calls per sequence):

 1. Create the sequence (name, description)

 2. Create sequence steps (timing between emails, auto vs manual send)

 3. Create email templates (subject + HTML body)

 4. Link templates to steps

 What needs to happen first:

 Your current Outreach OAuth app is read-only. We need to add write scopes and re-authorize. This means:

 1. You go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api and edit the Scout app registration

 2. Add these scopes: sequences.all , sequence Steps.all , templates.all , sequence Templates.all

 3. Optionally sequenceStates.all if you want Scout to also add prospects to sequences

 4. Re-authorize through the OAuth flow (the existing refresh token won't gain new scopes automatically)

 One question before you do that: Do you want sequences created as auto_email (sends automatically on the interval) or manual_email (creates a task in

 Outreach for you to review and send each one)? Given your "all sequences are drafts" rule, manual_email might be the safer starting point.

 Want to go update the OAuth scopes now? Once you re-authorize, I can build the sequence creation feature.

 ❯ I want the sequeneces created as auto_email and the first step should send after 5 minutes. I will reveiew all the sequences before we start adding people

 to the sequences and sending them out. We will need to talk through all the settings to get them optimized eventually.

 Walk me through exaclty what I need to do

 ⏺ Here's exactly what to do, step by step:

 Step 1: Update the OAuth app scopes

 1. Go to https://app.outreach.io/accounts/admin/api

 2. Find the Scout app (the one we set up in Session 34)

 3. Click edit/settings on it

 4. Find the scopes/permissions section

 5. Add these scopes (keep all existing read scopes too):

 - sequences.all

 - sequenceSteps.all

 - templates.all

 - sequenceTemplates.all

 - sequenceStates.all

 - mailboxes.read (may already be there)

 6. Save the app

 Step 2: Re-authorize

 After saving the updated scopes, you need to re-authorize so we get a new token with write permissions. Let me generate the authorization URL for you.

 ⏺ Bash (railway variables --json 2>&1 | python3 -c "

 import sys, json…)

 ⎿   Client ID: 5L~q8FE3w7yU2~WK.p On2qARfdd8m2f EoDFl~qTM28fa

 Redirect URI: https://web-production-1010b.up.railway.app/oauth/outreach/callback

 … +4 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ After you update the scopes in Step 1, do Step 2:

 1. Open this URL in your browser:

 https://api.outreach.io/oauth/authorize?client_id=5L~q8FE3w7yU2~WK.p On2qARfdd8m2f EoDFl~qTM28fa&redirect_uri=https://web-production-1010b.up.railway.app/oaut

 h/outreach/callback&response_type=code&scope=sequences.all+sequence Steps.all+templates.all+sequence Templates.all+sequence States.all+mailboxes.read+prospects

 .read+sequenceSteps.read+sequences.read+mailings.read+users.read

 2. Outreach will ask you to authorize — click Allow

 3. It'll redirect to the Railway callback URL, which should exchange the code for new tokens automatically

 Let me know when you've done both steps (or if you hit any snags on the Outreach admin page).

 ⏺ Your "Claude Token for Scout" Git Hub fine-grained personal access token has expired. This is the token Scout uses to persist memory to Git Hub ( GITHUB_TOKEN

 on Railway). If it's expired, Scout can't commit memory updates.

 You need to regenerate it:

 3. Make sure it still has contents:write permission on the scadkin/firstcocoagent repo

 4. Copy the new token

 5. Tell me the new token and I'll update it on Railway

 Do that first, then we'll come back to the Outreach setup.

 337 return resp.json()

 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :

 341 + """

 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).

 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 360 +

 361 +

 363 """

 676 + def get_mailboxes () -> list [ dict ]:

 677 + """Get available mailboxes for sending sequences."""

 678 + raw = _api_get_all( "/mailboxes" )

 679 + mailboxes = []

 680 + for item in raw:

 681 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 682 + mailboxes.append({

 683 + "id" : item.get( "id" ),

 684 + "email" : attrs.get( "email" , "" ),

 685 + "username" : attrs.get( "username" , "" ),

 686 + })

 687 + return mailboxes

 689 +

 690 + def create_sequence (

 691 + name : str ,

 692 + steps : list [ dict ],

 693 + description : str = "" ,

 694 + tags : list [ str ] | None = None ,

 695 + ) -> dict :

 696 + """

 697 + Create a complete sequence in Outreach with email steps.

 698 +

 699 + Args:

 700 + name: Sequence name (shown in Outreach UI)

 701 + steps: List of step dicts, each with:

 702 + - subject: Email subject line

 703 + - body_html: HTML email body

 704 + - interval_minutes: Minutes after previous step (first step = 5 min)

 705 + description: Optional sequence description

 706 + tags: Optional list of tags

 707 +

 708 + Returns dict with sequence details + step/template IDs, or error.

 709 +

 710 + Flow:

 711 + 1. Create sequence

 712 + 2. For each step: create template → create sequence Step → link via sequence Template

 713 + """

 714 + logger.info( f "Creating Outreach sequence: {name} ( { len (steps)} steps)" )

 715 +

 716 + # Step 1: Create the sequence

 717 + seq_attrs = {

 718 + "name" : name,

 719 + "sequenceType" : "interval" ,

 720 + }

 721 + if description:

 722 + seq_attrs[ "description" ] = description

 723 + if tags:

 724 + seq_attrs[ "tags" ] = tags

 725 +

 726 + seq_payload = {

 727 + "data" : {

 728 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 729 + "attributes" : seq_attrs,

 730 + }

 731 + }

 732 +

 733 + try :

 734 + seq_result = _api_post( "/sequences" , seq_payload)

 735 + except Exception as e:

 736 + return { "error" : f "Failed to create sequence: {e} " }

 737 +

 738 + seq_id = seq_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 739 + if not seq_id:

 740 + return { "error" : f "No sequence ID returned: {seq_result} " }

 741 +

 742 + logger.info( f " Created sequence ID: {seq_id} " )

 743 +

 744 + created_steps = []

 745 +

 746 + for i, step in enumerate (steps):

 747 + subject = step.get( "subject" , f "Step {i + 1 } " )

 748 + body_html = step.get( "body_html" , "" )

 749 + interval = step.get( "interval_minutes" , 5 if i == 0 else 1440 )

 750 +

 751 + # Step 2a: Create template

 752 + template_payload = {

 753 + "data" : {

 754 + "type" : "template" ,

 755 + "attributes" : {

 756 + "subject" : subject,

 757 + "bodyHtml" : body_html,

 758 + },

 759 + }

 760 + }

 761 +

 762 + try :

 763 + template_result = _api_post( "/templates" , template_payload)

 764 + template_id = template_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 765 + except Exception as e:

 766 + logger.error( f " Failed to create template for step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 767 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "error" : str (e)})

 768 + continue

 769 +

 770 + # Step 2b: Create sequence step

 771 + step_payload = {

 772 + "data" : {

 773 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 774 + "attributes" : {

 775 + "stepType" : "auto_email" ,

 776 + "interval" : interval,

 777 + "order" : i + 1 ,

 778 + },

 779 + "relationships" : {

 780 + "sequence" : {

 781 + "data" : {

 782 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 783 + "id" : int (seq_id),

 784 + }

 785 + }

 786 + },

 787 + }

 788 + }

 789 +

 790 + try :

 791 + step_result = _api_post( "/sequenceSteps" , step_payload)

 792 + step_id = step_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 793 + except Exception as e:

 794 + logger.error( f " Failed to create step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 795 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "template_id" : template_id, "error" : str (e)})

 796 + continue

 797 +

 798 + # Step 2c: Link template to step

 799 + link_payload = {

 800 + "data" : {

 801 + "type" : "sequenceTemplate" ,

 802 + "relationships" : {

 803 + "sequenceStep" : {

 804 + "data" : {

 805 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 806 + "id" : int (step_id),

 807 + }

 808 + },

 809 + "template" : {

 810 + "data" : {

 811 + "type" : "template" ,

 812 + "id" : int (template_id),

 813 + }

 814 + },

 815 + },

 816 + }

 817 + }

 818 +

 819 + try :

 820 + _api_post( "/sequenceTemplates" , link_payload)

 821 + except Exception as e:

 822 + logger.error( f " Failed to link template to step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 823 + created_steps.append({

 824 + "step" : i + 1 , "step_id" : step_id,

 825 + "template_id" : template_id, "error" : f "Link failed: {e} " ,

 826 + })

 827 + continue

 828 +

 829 + created_steps.append({

 830 + "step" : i + 1 ,

 831 + "step_id" : step_id,

 832 + "template_id" : template_id,

 833 + "subject" : subject,

 834 + "interval_minutes" : interval,

 835 + })

 836 + logger.info( f " Step {i + 1 } : template= {template_id} , step= {step_id} , interval= {interval} min, subject= {subject[: 50 ]} " )

 837 +

 838 + result = {

 839 + "sequence_id" : seq_id,

 840 + "name" : name,

 841 + "steps" : created_steps,

 842 + "errors" : [s for s in created_steps if "error" in s],

 843 + }

 844 +

 845 + if result[ "errors" ]:

 846 + logger.warning( f " Sequence created with { len (result[ 'errors' ])} errors" )

 847 + else :

 848 + logger.info( f " Sequence ' {name} ' created successfully with { len (created_steps)} steps" )

 849 +

 850 + return result

 Not currently — there's no API or integration between Claude Code and claude.ai web chats. They're isolated systems.

 I agree it would be useful though. For now, the best workaround is to copy the sequence content into a Google Doc and share the link with me, or just paste
 it here. That way I can read it and create the sequences directly in Outreach.

 Want to do that, or should I draft fresh CUE sequences from scratch?

 ⏺ Your "Claude Token for Scout" Git Hub fine-grained personal access token has expired. This is the token Scout uses to persist memory to Git Hub ( GITHUB_TOKEN

 on Railway). If it's expired, Scout can't commit memory updates.

 You need to regenerate it:

 1. Click "Regenerate your token" in that email (or go to https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/11872540/regenerate )

 2. Set a new expiration (I'd suggest 1 year this time so we don't hit this again soon)

 3. Make sure it still has contents:write permission on the scadkin/firstcocoagent repo

 4. Copy the new token

 5. Tell me the new token and I'll update it on Railway

 Do that first, then we'll come back to the Outreach setup.

 337 return resp.json()

 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :

 341 + """

 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).

 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 675 +

 676 + def get_mailboxes () -> list [ dict ]:

 677 + """Get available mailboxes for sending sequences."""

 678 + raw = _api_get_all( "/mailboxes" )

 679 + mailboxes = []

 680 + for item in raw:

 681 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 682 + mailboxes.append({

 683 + "id" : item.get( "id" ),

 684 + "email" : attrs.get( "email" , "" ),

 685 + "username" : attrs.get( "username" , "" ),

 686 + })

 687 + return mailboxes

 688 +

 689 +

 690 + def create_sequence (

 691 + name : str ,

 692 + steps : list [ dict ],

 693 + description : str = "" ,

 694 + tags : list [ str ] | None = None ,

 695 + ) -> dict :

 696 + """

 697 + Create a complete sequence in Outreach with email steps.

 698 +

 699 + Args:

 700 + name: Sequence name (shown in Outreach UI)

 701 + steps: List of step dicts, each with:

 702 + - subject: Email subject line

 703 + - body_html: HTML email body

 704 + - interval_minutes: Minutes after previous step (first step = 5 min)

 705 + description: Optional sequence description

 706 + tags: Optional list of tags

 707 +

 708 + Returns dict with sequence details + step/template IDs, or error.

 709 +

 710 + Flow:

 711 + 1. Create sequence

 712 + 2. For each step: create template → create sequence Step → link via sequence Template

 713 + """

 714 + logger.info( f "Creating Outreach sequence: {name} ( { len (steps)} steps)" )

 715 +

 716 + # Step 1: Create the sequence

 717 + seq_attrs = {

 718 + "name" : name,

 719 + "sequenceType" : "interval" ,

 720 + }

 721 + if description:

 722 + seq_attrs[ "description" ] = description

 723 + if tags:

 724 + seq_attrs[ "tags" ] = tags

 725 +

 726 + seq_payload = {

 727 + "data" : {

 728 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 729 + "attributes" : seq_attrs,

 730 + }

 731 + }

 732 +

 733 + try :

 734 + seq_result = _api_post( "/sequences" , seq_payload)

 735 + except Exception as e:

 736 + return { "error" : f "Failed to create sequence: {e} " }

 737 +

 738 + seq_id = seq_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 739 + if not seq_id:

 740 + return { "error" : f "No sequence ID returned: {seq_result} " }

 741 +

 742 + logger.info( f " Created sequence ID: {seq_id} " )

 743 +

 744 + created_steps = []

 745 +

 746 + for i, step in enumerate (steps):

 747 + subject = step.get( "subject" , f "Step {i + 1 } " )

 748 + body_html = step.get( "body_html" , "" )

 749 + interval = step.get( "interval_minutes" , 5 if i == 0 else 1440 )

 750 +

 751 + # Step 2a: Create template

 752 + template_payload = {

 753 + "data" : {

 754 + "type" : "template" ,

 755 + "attributes" : {

 756 + "subject" : subject,

 757 + "bodyHtml" : body_html,

 758 + },

 759 + }

 760 + }

 761 +

 762 + try :

 763 + template_result = _api_post( "/templates" , template_payload)

 764 + template_id = template_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 765 + except Exception as e:

 766 + logger.error( f " Failed to create template for step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 767 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "error" : str (e)})

 768 + continue

 769 +

 770 + # Step 2b: Create sequence step

 771 + step_payload = {

 772 + "data" : {

 773 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 774 + "attributes" : {

 775 + "stepType" : "auto_email" ,

 776 + "interval" : interval,

 777 + "order" : i + 1 ,

 778 + },

 779 + "relationships" : {

 780 + "sequence" : {

 781 + "data" : {

 782 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 783 + "id" : int (seq_id),

 784 + }

 785 + }

 786 + },

 787 + }

 788 + }

 789 +

 790 + try :

 791 + step_result = _api_post( "/sequenceSteps" , step_payload)

 792 + step_id = step_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 793 + except Exception as e:

 794 + logger.error( f " Failed to create step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 795 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "template_id" : template_id, "error" : str (e)})

 796 + continue

 797 +

 798 + # Step 2c: Link template to step

 799 + link_payload = {

 800 + "data" : {

 801 + "type" : "sequenceTemplate" ,

 802 + "relationships" : {

 803 + "sequenceStep" : {

 804 + "data" : {

 805 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 806 + "id" : int (step_id),

 807 + }

 808 + },

 809 + "template" : {

 810 + "data" : {

 811 + "type" : "template" ,

 812 + "id" : int (template_id),

 813 + }

 814 + },

 815 + },

 816 + }

 817 + }

 818 +

 819 + try :

 820 + _api_post( "/sequenceTemplates" , link_payload)

 821 + except Exception as e:

 822 + logger.error( f " Failed to link template to step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 823 + created_steps.append({

 824 + "step" : i + 1 , "step_id" : step_id,

 825 + "template_id" : template_id, "error" : f "Link failed: {e} " ,

 826 + })

 827 + continue

 828 +

 829 + created_steps.append({

 830 + "step" : i + 1 ,

 831 + "step_id" : step_id,

 832 + "template_id" : template_id,

 833 + "subject" : subject,

 834 + "interval_minutes" : interval,

 835 + })

 836 + logger.info( f " Step {i + 1 } : template= {template_id} , step= {step_id} , interval= {interval} min, subject= {subject[: 50 ]} " )

 837 +

 838 + result = {

 839 + "sequence_id" : seq_id,

 840 + "name" : name,

 841 + "steps" : created_steps,

 842 + "errors" : [s for s in created_steps if "error" in s],

 843 + }

 844 +

 845 + if result[ "errors" ]:

 846 + logger.warning( f " Sequence created with { len (result[ 'errors' ])} errors" )

 847 + else :

 848 + logger.info( f " Sequence ' {name} ' created successfully with { len (created_steps)} steps" )

 849 +

 850 + return result

 python3 << 'PYEOF'

 import os, json

 env_vars = {}

 with open('.env') as f:

 for line in f:

 line = line.strip()

 if not line or line.startswith('#') or '=' not in line: continue

 k, v = line.split('=', 1)

 env_vars[k.strip()] = v.strip()

 for k, v in env_vars.items():

 os.environ.setdefault(k, v)

 import tools.outreach_client as oc

 # Search for the referral/info dump template

 templates = oc._api_get_all("/templates", {"filter[name]": "New refferral"})

 if not templates:

 # Try broader search

 templates = oc._api_get_all("/templates", {"sort": "-updatedAt", "page[size]": "50"})

 for t in templates:

 name = t.get("attributes", {}).get("name", "")

 if "referral" in name.lower() or "refferral" in name.lower() or "info dump" in name.lower() or "2026" in name.lower():

 print(f"MATCH: ID={t['id']}: {name}")

 print(f"\nTotal templates: {len(templates)}")

 # Show recent ones

 print("\nRecent templates:")

 for t in templates[:10]:

 name = t.get("attributes", {}).get("name", "")

 print(f" ID={t['id']}: {name}")

 else:

 for t in templates:

 name = t.get("attributes", {}).get("name", "")

 print(f"FOUND: ID={t['id']}: {name}")

 import os, json

 <p>Most schools I'm working with are deep into fall planning right now and those budget conversations tend to close faster than anyone expects.</p>

 <p>If funding is one of the hurdles, we actually hired a Grant Writing Specialist this year who built some phenomenal free resources for educators.

 Pre-made templates, a hand-picked list of grants filtered by school type, and a recorded webinar that walks through the whole process step by step.</p>

 <p>Would it be helpful if I sent those over along with a quick quote so you have real numbers to bring to the table?</p>

 <p>Steven</p>"""

 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 # Create all 6 sequences

 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 results = {}

 for seq_name, seq_data in SEQUENCES.items():

 print(f"\n{'='*60}")

 print(f"Creating: {seq_name}")

 print(f"{'='*60}")

 result = oc.create_sequence(

 name=seq_name,

 steps=seq_data["steps"],

 description=seq_data.get("description", ""),

 tags=["CUE 2026", "booth"],

 )

 if "error" in result and not result.get("sequence_id"):

 print(f" FAILED: {result['error']}")

 results[seq_name] = result

 continue

 seq_id = result["sequence_id"]

 results[seq_name] = result

 # Now add Step 4 — link the existing template

 print(f" Adding Step 4 (existing template ID {EXISTING_TEMPLATE_ID})...")

 try:

 # Create step 4

 step4_payload = {

 "data": {

 "type": "sequenceStep",

 "attributes": {

 "stepType": "auto_email",

 "interval": STEP_4_INTERVAL,

 "order": 4,

 },

 "relationships": {

 "sequence": {

 "data": {"type": "sequence", "id": int(seq_id)}

 }

 },

 }

 }

 step4_result = oc._api_post("/sequenceSteps", step4_payload)

 step4_id = step4_result.get("data", {}).get("id")

 # Link existing template to step 4

 link_payload = {

 "data": {

 "type": "sequenceTemplate",

 "relationships": {

 "sequenceStep": {

 "data": {"type": "sequenceStep", "id": int(step4_id)}

 },

 "template": {

 "data": {"type": "template", "id": EXISTING_TEMPLATE_ID}

 },

 },

 }

 }

 oc._api_post("/sequenceTemplates", link_payload)

 print(f" Step 4 linked: step_id={step4_id}, template_id={EXISTING_TEMPLATE_ID}")

 result["steps"].append({

 "step": 4, "step_id": step4_id,

 "template_id": EXISTING_TEMPLATE_ID,

 "subject": "(existing template)",

 "interval_minutes": STEP_4_INTERVAL,

 })

 except Exception as e:

 print(f" Step 4 error: {e}")

 print("\n" + "="*60)

 print("SUMMARY")

 print("="*60)

 for name, result in results.items():

 seq_id = result.get("sequence_id", "FAILED")

 errors = result.get("errors", [])

 steps = len([s for s in result.get("steps", []) if "error" not in s])

 status = "OK" if not errors else f"{len(errors)} errors"

 print(f" {name}: seq_id={seq_id}, {steps} steps, {status}")

 ⏺ Your "Claude Token for Scout" Git Hub fine-grained personal access token has expired. This is the token Scout uses to persist memory to Git Hub ( GITHUB_TOKEN

 on Railway). If it's expired, Scout can't commit memory updates.

 You need to regenerate it:

 1. Click "Regenerate your token" in that email (or go to https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/11872540/regenerate )

 2. Set a new expiration (I'd suggest 1 year this time so we don't hit this again soon)

 3. Make sure it still has contents:write permission on the scadkin/firstcocoagent repo

 4. Copy the new token

 5. Tell me the new token and I'll update it on Railway

 Do that first, then we'll come back to the Outreach setup.

 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :

 341 + """

 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).

 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 360 +

 361 +

 363 """

 364 Paginate through all results for a GET endpoint.

 676 + def get_mailboxes () -> list [ dict ]:

 677 + """Get available mailboxes for sending sequences."""

 678 + raw = _api_get_all( "/mailboxes" )

 679 + mailboxes = []

 680 + for item in raw:

 681 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 682 + mailboxes.append({

 683 + "id" : item.get( "id" ),

 684 + "email" : attrs.get( "email" , "" ),

 685 + "username" : attrs.get( "username" , "" ),

 686 + })

 687 + return mailboxes

 688 +

 689 +

 690 + def create_sequence (

 691 + name : str ,

 692 + steps : list [ dict ],

 693 + description : str = "" ,

 694 + tags : list [ str ] | None = None ,

 695 + ) -> dict :

 696 + """

 697 + Create a complete sequence in Outreach with email steps.

 698 +

 699 + Args:

 700 + name: Sequence name (shown in Outreach UI)

 701 + steps: List of step dicts, each with:

 702 + - subject: Email subject line

 703 + - body_html: HTML email body

 704 + - interval_minutes: Minutes after previous step (first step = 5 min)

 705 + description: Optional sequence description

 706 + tags: Optional list of tags

 707 +

 708 + Returns dict with sequence details + step/template IDs, or error.

 709 +

 710 + Flow:

 711 + 1. Create sequence

 712 + 2. For each step: create template → create sequence Step → link via sequence Template

 713 + """

 714 + logger.info( f "Creating Outreach sequence: {name} ( { len (steps)} steps)" )

 715 +

 716 + # Step 1: Create the sequence

 717 + seq_attrs = {

 718 + "name" : name,

 719 + "sequenceType" : "interval" ,

 720 + }

 721 + if description:

 722 + seq_attrs[ "description" ] = description

 723 + if tags:

 724 + seq_attrs[ "tags" ] = tags

 725 +

 726 + seq_payload = {

 727 + "data" : {

 728 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 729 + "attributes" : seq_attrs,

 730 + }

 731 + }

 732 +

 733 + try :

 734 + seq_result = _api_post( "/sequences" , seq_payload)

 735 + except Exception as e:

 736 + return { "error" : f "Failed to create sequence: {e} " }

 737 +

 738 + seq_id = seq_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 739 + if not seq_id:

 740 + return { "error" : f "No sequence ID returned: {seq_result} " }

 741 +

 742 + logger.info( f " Created sequence ID: {seq_id} " )

 743 +

 744 + created_steps = []

 745 +

 746 + for i, step in enumerate (steps):

 747 + subject = step.get( "subject" , f "Step {i + 1 } " )

 748 + body_html = step.get( "body_html" , "" )

 749 + interval = step.get( "interval_minutes" , 5 if i == 0 else 1440 )

 750 +

 751 + # Step 2a: Create template

 752 + template_payload = {

 753 + "data" : {

 754 + "type" : "template" ,

 755 + "attributes" : {

 756 + "subject" : subject,

 757 + "bodyHtml" : body_html,

 758 + },

 759 + }

 760 + }

 761 +

 762 + try :

 763 + template_result = _api_post( "/templates" , template_payload)

 764 + template_id = template_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 765 + except Exception as e:

 766 + logger.error( f " Failed to create template for step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 767 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "error" : str (e)})

 768 + continue

 769 +

 770 + # Step 2b: Create sequence step

 771 + step_payload = {

 772 + "data" : {

 773 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 774 + "attributes" : {

 775 + "stepType" : "auto_email" ,

 776 + "interval" : interval,

 777 + "order" : i + 1 ,

 778 + },

 779 + "relationships" : {

 780 + "sequence" : {

 781 + "data" : {

 782 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 783 + "id" : int (seq_id),

 784 + }

 785 + }

 786 + },

 787 + }

 788 + }

 789 +

 790 + try :

 791 + step_result = _api_post( "/sequenceSteps" , step_payload)

 792 + step_id = step_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 793 + except Exception as e:

 794 + logger.error( f " Failed to create step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 795 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "template_id" : template_id, "error" : str (e)})

 796 + continue

 797 +

 798 + # Step 2c: Link template to step

 799 + link_payload = {

 800 + "data" : {

 801 + "type" : "sequenceTemplate" ,

 802 + "relationships" : {

 803 + "sequenceStep" : {

 804 + "data" : {

 805 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 806 + "id" : int (step_id),

 807 + }

 808 + },

 809 + "template" : {

 810 + "data" : {

 811 + "type" : "template" ,

 812 + "id" : int (template_id),

 813 + }

 814 + },

 815 + },

 816 + }

 817 + }

 818 +

 819 + try :

 820 + _api_post( "/sequenceTemplates" , link_payload)

 821 + except Exception as e:

 822 + logger.error( f " Failed to link template to step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 823 + created_steps.append({

 824 + "step" : i + 1 , "step_id" : step_id,

 825 + "template_id" : template_id, "error" : f "Link failed: {e} " ,

 826 + })

 827 + continue

 828 +

 829 + created_steps.append({

 830 + "step" : i + 1 ,

 831 + "step_id" : step_id,

 832 + "template_id" : template_id,

 833 + "subject" : subject,

 846 + logger.warning( f " Sequence created with { len (result[ 'errors' ])} errors" )

 Bash command

 python3 << 'PYEOF'

 import os, json, httpx

 env_vars = {}

 with open('.env') as f:

 for line in f:

 line = line.strip()

 if not line or line.startswith('#') or '=' not in line: continue

 k, v = line.split('=', 1)

 env_vars[k.strip()] = v.strip()

 for k, v in env_vars.items():

 os.environ.setdefault(k, v)

 LINK = 'https://hello.codecombat.com/c/steven/t/124'

 def br_email(*paragraphs):

 """Join paragraphs with <br><br> for natural email spacing."""

 return "<br><br>".join(paragraphs)

 }

 updated = 0

 for template_id, new_body in UPDATES.items():

 payload = {

 "data": {

 "type": "template",

 "id": int(template_id),

 "attributes": {

 "bodyHtml": new_body,

 },

 }

 }

 try:

 url = f"{oc.API_BASE}/templates/{template_id}"

 headers = oc._get_headers()

 resp = httpx.patch(url, headers=headers, json=payload, timeout=30.0)

 if resp.status_code in (200, 201):

 updated += 1

 else:

 print(f" ❌ template {template_id}: HTTP {resp.status_code}")

 except Exception as e:

 print(f" ❌ template {template_id}: {e}")

 print(f"✅ Updated {updated}/{len(UPDATES)} templates with proper paragraph spacing")

 ⏺ Your "Claude Token for Scout" Git Hub fine-grained personal access token has expired. This is the token Scout uses to persist memory to Git Hub ( GITHUB_TOKEN

 on Railway). If it's expired, Scout can't commit memory updates.

 2. Set a new expiration (I'd suggest 1 year this time so we don't hit this again soon)

 3. Make sure it still has contents:write permission on the scadkin/firstcocoagent repo

 4. Copy the new token

 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :

 341 + """

 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).

 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 360 +

 361 +

 362 def _api_get_all ( path : str , params : dict | None = None , max_pages : int = 50 ) -> list :

 363 """

 364 Paginate through all results for a GET endpoint.

 668 })

 669 return sorted (steps, key =lambda s : s.get( "order" , 0 ))

 672 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 673 + # WRITE METHODS — Sequence Creation (Session 38+)

 675 +

 676 + def get_mailboxes () -> list [ dict ]:

 677 + """Get available mailboxes for sending sequences."""

 678 + raw = _api_get_all( "/mailboxes" )

 679 + mailboxes = []

 680 + for item in raw:

 681 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 682 + mailboxes.append({

 683 + "id" : item.get( "id" ),

 684 + "email" : attrs.get( "email" , "" ),

 685 + "username" : attrs.get( "username" , "" ),

 686 + })

 687 + return mailboxes

 688 +

 689 +

 690 + def create_sequence (

 691 + name : str ,

 692 + steps : list [ dict ],

 693 + description : str = "" ,

 694 + tags : list [ str ] | None = None ,

 695 + ) -> dict :

 696 + """

 697 + Create a complete sequence in Outreach with email steps.

 698 +

 699 + Args:

 700 + name: Sequence name (shown in Outreach UI)

 701 + steps: List of step dicts, each with:

 702 + - subject: Email subject line

 703 + - body_html: HTML email body

 704 + - interval_minutes: Minutes after previous step (first step = 5 min)

 705 + description: Optional sequence description

 706 + tags: Optional list of tags

 707 +

 708 + Returns dict with sequence details + step/template IDs, or error.

 709 +

 710 + Flow:

 711 + 1. Create sequence

 712 + 2. For each step: create template → create sequence Step → link via sequence Template

 713 + """

 714 + logger.info( f "Creating Outreach sequence: {name} ( { len (steps)} steps)" )

 715 +

 716 + # Step 1: Create the sequence

 717 + seq_attrs = {

 718 + "name" : name,

 719 + "sequenceType" : "interval" ,

 720 + }

 721 + if description:

 722 + seq_attrs[ "description" ] = description

 723 + if tags:

 724 + seq_attrs[ "tags" ] = tags

 725 +

 726 + seq_payload = {

 727 + "data" : {

 728 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 729 + "attributes" : seq_attrs,

 730 + }

 731 + }

 732 +

 733 + try :

 734 + seq_result = _api_post( "/sequences" , seq_payload)

 735 + except Exception as e:

 736 + return { "error" : f "Failed to create sequence: {e} " }

 737 +

 738 + seq_id = seq_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 739 + if not seq_id:

 740 + return { "error" : f "No sequence ID returned: {seq_result} " }

 741 +

 742 + logger.info( f " Created sequence ID: {seq_id} " )

 743 +

 744 + created_steps = []

 745 +

 746 + for i, step in enumerate (steps):

 747 + subject = step.get( "subject" , f "Step {i + 1 } " )

 748 + body_html = step.get( "body_html" , "" )

 749 + interval = step.get( "interval_minutes" , 5 if i == 0 else 1440 )

 750 +

 751 + # Step 2a: Create template

 752 + template_payload = {

 753 + "data" : {

 754 + "type" : "template" ,

 755 + "attributes" : {

 756 + "subject" : subject,

 757 + "bodyHtml" : body_html,

 758 + },

 759 + }

 760 + }

 761 +

 762 + try :

 763 + template_result = _api_post( "/templates" , template_payload)

 764 + template_id = template_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 765 + except Exception as e:

 766 + logger.error( f " Failed to create template for step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 767 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "error" : str (e)})

 768 + continue

 769 +

 770 + # Step 2b: Create sequence step

 771 + step_payload = {

 772 + "data" : {

 773 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 774 + "attributes" : {

 775 + "stepType" : "auto_email" ,

 776 + "interval" : interval,

 777 + "order" : i + 1 ,

 778 + },

 779 + "relationships" : {

 780 + "sequence" : {

 781 + "data" : {

 782 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 783 + "id" : int (seq_id),

 784 + }

 785 + }

 786 + },

 787 + }

 788 + }

 789 +

 790 + try :

 791 + step_result = _api_post( "/sequenceSteps" , step_payload)

 792 + step_id = step_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 793 + except Exception as e:

 794 + logger.error( f " Failed to create step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 795 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "template_id" : template_id, "error" : str (e)})

 796 + continue

 797 +

 798 + # Step 2c: Link template to step

 799 + link_payload = {

 800 + "data" : {

 801 + "type" : "sequenceTemplate" ,

 802 + "relationships" : {

 803 + "sequenceStep" : {

 804 + "data" : {

 805 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 806 + "id" : int (step_id),

 807 + }

 808 + },

 809 + "template" : {

 810 + "data" : {

 811 + "type" : "template" ,

 812 + "id" : int (template_id),

 813 + }

 814 + },

 815 + },

 816 + }

 817 + }

 818 +

 819 + try :

 820 + _api_post( "/sequenceTemplates" , link_payload)

 821 + except Exception as e:

 822 + logger.error( f " Failed to link template to step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 823 + created_steps.append({

 824 + "step" : i + 1 , "step_id" : step_id,

 825 + "template_id" : template_id, "error" : f "Link failed: {e} " ,

 826 + })

 827 + continue

 828 +

 829 + created_steps.append({

 830 + "step" : i + 1 ,

 831 + "step_id" : step_id,

 832 + "template_id" : template_id,

 833 + "subject" : subject,

 834 + "interval_minutes" : interval,

 835 + })

 836 + logger.info( f " Step {i + 1 } : template= {template_id} , step= {step_id} , interval= {interval} min, subject= {subject[: 50 ]} " )

 837 +

 838 + result = {

 839 + "sequence_id" : seq_id,

 840 + "name" : name,

 841 + "steps" : created_steps,

 842 + "errors" : [s for s in created_steps if "error" in s],

 843 + }

 844 +

 845 + if result[ "errors" ]:

 846 + logger.warning( f " Sequence created with { len (result[ 'errors' ])} errors" )

 847 + else :

 848 + logger.info( f " Sequence ' {name} ' created successfully with { len (created_steps)} steps" )

 849 +

 850 + return result

 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :

 341 + """

 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).

 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 360 +

 361 +

 668 })

 669 return sorted (steps, key =lambda s : s.get( "order" , 0 ))

 672 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 673 + # WRITE METHODS — Sequence Creation (Session 38+)

 674 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 675 +

 676 + def get_mailboxes () -> list [ dict ]:

 677 + """Get available mailboxes for sending sequences."""

 678 + raw = _api_get_all( "/mailboxes" )

 679 + mailboxes = []

 680 + for item in raw:

 681 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 682 + mailboxes.append({

 683 + "id" : item.get( "id" ),

 684 + "email" : attrs.get( "email" , "" ),

 685 + "username" : attrs.get( "username" , "" ),

 686 + })

 687 + return mailboxes

 688 +

 689 +

 690 + def create_sequence (

 691 + name : str ,

 692 + steps : list [ dict ],

 693 + description : str = "" ,

 694 + tags : list [ str ] | None = None ,

 695 + ) -> dict :

 696 + """

 697 + Create a complete sequence in Outreach with email steps.

 698 +

 699 + Args:

 700 + name: Sequence name (shown in Outreach UI)

 701 + steps: List of step dicts, each with:

 702 + - subject: Email subject line

 703 + - body_html: HTML email body

 704 + - interval_minutes: Minutes after previous step (first step = 5 min)

 705 + description: Optional sequence description

 706 + tags: Optional list of tags

 707 +

 708 + Returns dict with sequence details + step/template IDs, or error.

 709 +

 710 + Flow:

 711 + 1. Create sequence

 712 + 2. For each step: create template → create sequence Step → link via sequence Template

 713 + """

 714 + logger.info( f "Creating Outreach sequence: {name} ( { len (steps)} steps)" )

 715 +

 716 + # Step 1: Create the sequence

 717 + seq_attrs = {

 718 + "name" : name,

 719 + "sequenceType" : "interval" ,

 720 + }

 721 + if description:

 722 + seq_attrs[ "description" ] = description

 723 + if tags:

 724 + seq_attrs[ "tags" ] = tags

 725 +

 726 + seq_payload = {

 727 + "data" : {

 728 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 729 + "attributes" : seq_attrs,

 730 + }

 731 + }

 732 +

 733 + try :

 734 + seq_result = _api_post( "/sequences" , seq_payload)

 735 + except Exception as e:

 736 + return { "error" : f "Failed to create sequence: {e} " }

 737 +

 738 + seq_id = seq_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 739 + if not seq_id:

 740 + return { "error" : f "No sequence ID returned: {seq_result} " }

 741 +

 742 + logger.info( f " Created sequence ID: {seq_id} " )

 743 +

 744 + created_steps = []

 745 +

 746 + for i, step in enumerate (steps):

 747 + subject = step.get( "subject" , f "Step {i + 1 } " )

 748 + body_html = step.get( "body_html" , "" )

 749 + interval = step.get( "interval_minutes" , 5 if i == 0 else 1440 )

 750 +

 751 + # Step 2a: Create template

 752 + template_payload = {

 753 + "data" : {

 754 + "type" : "template" ,

 755 + "attributes" : {

 756 + "subject" : subject,

 757 + "bodyHtml" : body_html,

 758 + },

 759 + }

 760 + }

 761 +

 762 + try :

 763 + template_result = _api_post( "/templates" , template_payload)

 764 + template_id = template_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 765 + except Exception as e:

 766 + logger.error( f " Failed to create template for step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 767 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "error" : str (e)})

 768 + continue

 769 +

 770 + # Step 2b: Create sequence step

 771 + step_payload = {

 772 + "data" : {

 773 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 774 + "attributes" : {

 775 + "stepType" : "auto_email" ,

 776 + "interval" : interval,

 777 + "order" : i + 1 ,

 778 + },

 779 + "relationships" : {

 780 + "sequence" : {

 781 + "data" : {

 782 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 783 + "id" : int (seq_id),

 784 + }

 785 + }

 786 + },

 787 + }

 788 + }

 789 +

 790 + try :

 791 + step_result = _api_post( "/sequenceSteps" , step_payload)

 792 + step_id = step_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 793 + except Exception as e:

 794 + logger.error( f " Failed to create step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 795 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "template_id" : template_id, "error" : str (e)})

 796 + continue

 797 +

 798 + # Step 2c: Link template to step

 799 + link_payload = {

 800 + "data" : {

 801 + "type" : "sequenceTemplate" ,

 802 + "relationships" : {

 803 + "sequenceStep" : {

 804 + "data" : {

 805 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 806 + "id" : int (step_id),

 807 + }

 808 + },

 809 + "template" : {

 810 + "data" : {

 811 + "type" : "template" ,

 812 + "id" : int (template_id),

 813 + }

 814 + },

 815 + },

 816 + }

 817 + }

 818 +

 819 + try :

 820 + _api_post( "/sequenceTemplates" , link_payload)

 821 + except Exception as e:

 822 + logger.error( f " Failed to link template to step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 823 + created_steps.append({

 824 + "step" : i + 1 , "step_id" : step_id,

 825 + "template_id" : template_id, "error" : f "Link failed: {e} " ,

 826 + })

 827 + continue

 828 +

 829 + created_steps.append({

 830 + "step" : i + 1 ,

 831 + "step_id" : step_id,

 832 + "template_id" : template_id,

 833 + "subject" : subject,

 834 + "interval_minutes" : interval,

 835 + })

 836 + logger.info( f " Step {i + 1 } : template= {template_id} , step= {step_id} , interval= {interval} min, subject= {subject[: 50 ]} " )

 837 +

 838 + result = {

 839 + "sequence_id" : seq_id,

 840 + "name" : name,

 841 + "steps" : created_steps,

 842 + "errors" : [s for s in created_steps if "error" in s],

 843 + }

 844 +

 845 + if result[ "errors" ]:

 846 + logger.warning( f " Sequence created with { len (result[ 'errors' ])} errors" )

 847 + else :

 848 + logger.info( f " Sequence ' {name} ' created successfully with { len (created_steps)} steps" )

 849 +

 850 + return result

 d+sequenceTemplates.write+templates.read+templates.write+users.read

 Bash command

 python3 << 'PYEOF'

 import os, json, httpx

 env_vars = {}

 with open('.env') as f:

 for line in f:

 line = line.strip()

 if not line or line.startswith('#') or '=' not in line: continue

 k, v = line.split('=', 1)

 env_vars[k.strip()] = v.strip()

 for k, v in env_vars.items():

 os.environ.setdefault(k, v)

 import tools.outreach_client as oc

 # Try the correct endpoint — might need /sequenceStates/ID with PATCH

 # Let's first check what the API docs say by trying different approaches

 state_id = 512309 # one of the paused ones

 # Approach 1: Try with full URL path

 headers = oc._get_headers()

 payload = {

 "data": {

 "type": "sequenceState",

 "id": state_id,

 "attributes": {

 "state": "active",

 },

 }

 }

 # Try PATCH /api/v2/sequenceStates/{id}

 resp = httpx.patch(f"https://api.outreach.io/api/v2/sequenceStates/{state_id}",

 headers=headers, json=payload, timeout=30.0)

 print(f"PATCH /sequenceStates/{state_id}: {resp.status_code}")

 print(f" {resp.text[:300]}")

 Debug sequenceState PATCH endpoint

 Command contains quote characters inside a # comment which can desync quote tracking

 - e inference → Serper web search (school name + email) → district enrichment. Final: 1,274 targets, 113 empty states, 100% district coverage,

 10 - ### What was done (Session 37 )

 12 - - **Pattern extraction improvements:** Education keyword stripping (kyschools→KY), expanded city/county map (30+ entries)

 - aping if no Serper key.

 15 - - **Parent district enrichment:** NCES re-matching with known state + Serper extraction. 100% coverage.

 16 - - **Deterministic international detection:** TLDs, foreign edu domains, school name keywords, search content signals. No longer depends on Cl

 - aude's nondeterministic `is_us` flag.

 18 - - **API cost tracking:** Shows estimated cost in `/c4` completion message

 + `/todo_remove` ), Claude tool ( `manage_todos` ), hourly check-ins now reference open todos.

 12 + - **CUE 2026 lead enrichment:** `scripts/enrich_cue_leads.py` — offline script. 1,472 raw → 1,298 deduped. 5-layer pipeline: email domain par

 + sing → SF domain→state lookup (859 roots) → Serper web search (parallel, deduped) → Claude extraction from search results → Claude inference

 + for remaining. NCES name normalization against 8,038 districts + 36,145 schools. Rep routing tabs (Steven/Tom/Liz/Shan/CUE-CALIE). Output to

 + Google Sheet + CSV.

 13 + - **Outreach API write access:** Re-authorized OAuth with write scopes for sequences, sequence Steps, sequence States, sequence Templates, templ

 + ates, prospects. Ready to build sequence creation feature.

 14 + - **Session transcript numbering fix:** `scout_session.sh` now checks `.raw/` files + cross-references CLAUDE.md for correct session number.

 15 + - **GitHub token regenerated:** Fine-grained PAT was expired, regenerated with 1-year expiry.

 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :

 341 + """

 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).

 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 360 +

 361 +

 362 def _api_get_all ( path : str , params : dict | None = None , max_pages : int = 50 ) -> list :

 363 """

 364 Paginate through all results for a GET endpoint.

 667 "name" : attrs.get( "name" , "" ),

 668 })

 669 return sorted (steps, key =lambda s : s.get( "order" , 0 ))

 672 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 673 + # WRITE METHODS — Sequence Creation (Session 38+)

 674 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 675 +

 676 + def get_mailboxes () -> list [ dict ]:

 677 + """Get available mailboxes for sending sequences."""

 678 + raw = _api_get_all( "/mailboxes" )

 679 + mailboxes = []

 680 + for item in raw:

 681 + attrs = item.get( "attributes" , {})

 682 + mailboxes.append({

 683 + "id" : item.get( "id" ),

 684 + "email" : attrs.get( "email" , "" ),

 685 + "username" : attrs.get( "username" , "" ),

 686 + })

 687 + return mailboxes

 688 +

 689 +

 690 + def create_sequence (

 691 + name : str ,

 692 + steps : list [ dict ],

 693 + description : str = "" ,

 694 + tags : list [ str ] | None = None ,

 695 + ) -> dict :

 696 + """

 697 + Create a complete sequence in Outreach with email steps.

 698 +

 699 + Args:

 700 + name: Sequence name (shown in Outreach UI)

 701 + steps: List of step dicts, each with:

 702 + - subject: Email subject line

 703 + - body_html: HTML email body

 704 + - interval_minutes: Minutes after previous step (first step = 5 min)

 705 + description: Optional sequence description

 706 + tags: Optional list of tags

 707 +

 708 + Returns dict with sequence details + step/template IDs, or error.

 709 +

 710 + Flow:

 711 + 1. Create sequence

 712 + 2. For each step: create template → create sequence Step → link via sequence Template

 713 + """

 714 + logger.info( f "Creating Outreach sequence: {name} ( { len (steps)} steps)" )

 715 +

 716 + # Step 1: Create the sequence

 717 + seq_attrs = {

 718 + "name" : name,

 719 + "sequenceType" : "interval" ,

 720 + }

 721 + if description:

 722 + seq_attrs[ "description" ] = description

 723 + if tags:

 724 + seq_attrs[ "tags" ] = tags

 725 +

 726 + seq_payload = {

 727 + "data" : {

 728 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 729 + "attributes" : seq_attrs,

 730 + }

 731 + }

 732 +

 733 + try :

 734 + seq_result = _api_post( "/sequences" , seq_payload)

 735 + except Exception as e:

 736 + return { "error" : f "Failed to create sequence: {e} " }

 737 +

 738 + seq_id = seq_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 739 + if not seq_id:

 740 + return { "error" : f "No sequence ID returned: {seq_result} " }

 741 +

 742 + logger.info( f " Created sequence ID: {seq_id} " )

 743 +

 744 + created_steps = []

 745 +

 746 + for i, step in enumerate (steps):

 747 + subject = step.get( "subject" , f "Step {i + 1 } " )

 748 + body_html = step.get( "body_html" , "" )

 749 + interval = step.get( "interval_minutes" , 5 if i == 0 else 1440 )

 750 +

 751 + # Step 2a: Create template

 752 + template_payload = {

 753 + "data" : {

 754 + "type" : "template" ,

 755 + "attributes" : {

 756 + "subject" : subject,

 757 + "bodyHtml" : body_html,

 758 + },

 759 + }

 760 + }

 761 +

 762 + try :

 763 + template_result = _api_post( "/templates" , template_payload)

 764 + template_id = template_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 765 + except Exception as e:

 766 + logger.error( f " Failed to create template for step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 767 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "error" : str (e)})

 768 + continue

 769 +

 770 + # Step 2b: Create sequence step

 771 + step_payload = {

 772 + "data" : {

 773 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 774 + "attributes" : {

 775 + "stepType" : "auto_email" ,

 776 + "interval" : interval,

 777 + "order" : i + 1 ,

 778 + },

 779 + "relationships" : {

 780 + "sequence" : {

 781 + "data" : {

 782 + "type" : "sequence" ,

 783 + "id" : int (seq_id),

 784 + }

 785 + }

 786 + },

 787 + }

 788 + }

 789 +

 790 + try :

 791 + step_result = _api_post( "/sequenceSteps" , step_payload)

 792 + step_id = step_result.get( "data" , {}).get( "id" )

 793 + except Exception as e:

 794 + logger.error( f " Failed to create step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 795 + created_steps.append({ "step" : i + 1 , "template_id" : template_id, "error" : str (e)})

 796 + continue

 797 +

 798 + # Step 2c: Link template to step

 799 + link_payload = {

 800 + "data" : {

 801 + "type" : "sequenceTemplate" ,

 802 + "relationships" : {

 803 + "sequenceStep" : {

 804 + "data" : {

 805 + "type" : "sequenceStep" ,

 806 + "id" : int (step_id),

 807 + }

 808 + },

 809 + "template" : {

 810 + "data" : {

 811 + "type" : "template" ,

 812 + "id" : int (template_id),

 813 + }

 814 + },

 815 + },

 816 + }

 817 + }

 818 +

 819 + try :

 820 + _api_post( "/sequenceTemplates" , link_payload)

 821 + except Exception as e:

 822 + logger.error( f " Failed to link template to step {i + 1 } : {e} " )

 823 + created_steps.append({

 824 + "step" : i + 1 , "step_id" : step_id,

 825 + "template_id" : template_id, "error" : f "Link failed: {e} " ,

 826 + })

 827 + continue

 828 +

 829 + created_steps.append({

 830 + "step" : i + 1 ,

 831 + "step_id" : step_id,

 832 + "template_id" : template_id,

 833 + "subject" : subject,

 834 + "interval_minutes" : interval,

 835 + })

 836 + logger.info( f " Step {i + 1 } : template= {template_id} , step= {step_id} , interval= {interval} min, subject= {subject[: 50 ]} " )

 837 +

 838 + result = {

 839 + "sequence_id" : seq_id,

 840 + "name" : name,

 841 + "steps" : created_steps,

 842 + "errors" : [s for s in created_steps if "error" in s],

 843 + }

 844 +

 845 + if result[ "errors" ]:

 846 + logger.warning( f " Sequence created with { len (result[ 'errors' ])} errors" )

 847 + else :

 848 + logger.info( f " Sequence ' {name} ' created successfully with { len (created_steps)} steps" )

 849 +

 850 + return result

 ⏺ The Outreach API doesn't support PATCH on sequenceStates — it's a read-only + create-only resource. Prospects get added as "paused" and need to be resumed
 from the Outreach UI.

 For the paused prospects: You'll need to resume them in Outreach. The fastest way is to go into each sequence, select all prospects, and click "Resume" in
 bulk. It's a few clicks per sequence.

 For future sequences: I can add prospects with state: "active" on creation. Let me check if that works.

 ⏺ Bash (python3 << 'PYEOF'
 import os, json, httpx…)
 ⎿  Running…

 Bash command

 python3 << 'PYEOF'

 import os, json, httpx

 env_vars = {}

 with open('.env') as f:

 for line in f:

 line = line.strip()

 if not line or line.startswith('#') or '=' not in line: continue

 k, v = line.split('=', 1)

 env_vars[k.strip()] = v.strip()

 for k, v in env_vars.items():

 os.environ.setdefault(k, v)

 import tools.outreach_client as oc

 # Check: can we get Samantha's sequenceState (just added) and see its state?

 states = oc._api_get_all("/sequenceStates", {

 "filter[sequence][id]": "1987", # Admin sequence

 "filter[state]": "paused",

 })

 print(f"Paused in Admin seq: {len(states)}")

 # Check if sequenceState creation supports setting initial state

 # Try to get the schema/options

 for s in states:

 attrs = s.get("attributes", {})

 prospect_ref = s.get("relationships", {}).get("prospect", {}).get("data", {})

 print(f" ID={s['id']}, state={attrs.get('state')}, prospect={prospect_ref.get('id')}")

 Check sequenceState status

 Command contains quote characters inside a # comment which can desync quote tracking

 ⏺ Unfortunately the Outreach API doesn't let us change sequence state from paused to active — that's controlled through the UI only.

 You'll need to resume them manually. Fastest way: in each sequence, click the "Paused" count, select all, then "Resume." Six sequences, about 30 seconds
 total.

 For Samantha Bores — she's been added successfully (she'll show as paused too, so resume her along with the others in the Admin sequence).

 For future sequences , I'll note this limitation. The API adds prospects as paused — you activate them in the UI after review. That's actually not a bad
 workflow since you want to review before sending anyway.

 ?

 ❯ what is the easiest way for me to resume all of these prospect across all these sequences?

 ⏺ Go into each sequence, click the orange "Paused" number, then:

 1. Select all (checkbox at top)
 2. Click "Resume" in the bulk action bar

 6 sequences, about 10 seconds each. That's the fastest way — Outreach doesn't have a "resume all across all sequences" button unfortunately.

 Alternatively, if there's a "Prospects" view in Outreach where you can filter by sequence tag "CUE 2026" + status "Paused", you might be able to bulk resume
 all 59 at once from there.

 a é

 ⏺ She was the last one added — after you already activated the sequences and toggled the templates on. The earlier 58 prospects were added before the
 sequences were active, so they went in as paused. Samantha was added after everything was live, so she went straight to active.

 Good to know — for future sequences, the order should be:

 1. Create sequences + steps + templates
 2. Activate sequences in UI
 3. Toggle templates on in UI

 4. Then add prospects via API → they'll go in as active

 I'll build it that way next time so you don't have to manually resume.

 import os, json, csv…)
 ⎿  Running…

 Bash command

 python3 << 'PYEOF'

 import os, json, csv

 env_vars = {}

 with open('.env') as f:

 for line in f:

 line = line.strip()

 if not line or line.startswith('#') or '=' not in line: continue

 k, v = line.split('=', 1)

 env_vars[k.strip()] = v.strip()

 for k, v in env_vars.items():

 os.environ.setdefault(k, v)

 from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials

 from googleapiclient.discovery import build

 creds = Credentials.from_service_account_info(

 json.loads(os.environ['GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON']),

 scopes=["https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets.readonly"],

 )

 service = build("sheets", "v4", credentials=creds)

 sheet_id = os.environ['GOOGLE_SHEETS_ID']

 # Load Active Accounts

 result = service.spreadsheets().values().get(

 spreadsheetId=sheet_id, range="'Active Accounts'!A1:ZZ"

 ).execute()

 rows = result.get("values", [])

 if not rows:

 print("No Active Accounts data")

 exit()

 headers = rows[0]

 accounts = []

 for row in rows[1:]:

 padded = row + [""] * (len(headers) - len(row))

 accounts.append(dict(zip(headers, padded)))

 # Build sets of active account names and email domains

 active_names = set()

 active_domains = set()

 for acc in accounts:

 name = (acc.get("Active Account Name", "") or acc.get("Display Name", "")).lower().strip()

 if name:

 active_names.add(name)

 # Also check parent account

 parent = acc.get("Parent Account", "").lower().strip()

 if parent:

 active_names.add(parent)

 print(f"Active Accounts: {len(accounts)} rows, {len(active_names)} unique names")

 # Load CUE leads

 with open('/Users/stevenadkins/Downloads/CUE_2026_Leads_Segmented.csv') as f:

 reader = csv.DictReader(f)

 leads = [r for r in reader if r.get("Segment") != "FLAGGED"]

 print(f"CUE booth leads: {len(leads)}")

 print()

 # Cross-check: does the lead's company/district match any active account?

 matches = []

 for lead in leads:

 company = lead.get("Company", "").lower().strip()

 email = lead.get("Email", "").strip()

 name = f"{lead.get('First Name', '')} {lead.get('Last Name', '')}".strip()

 matched_account = None

 # Check company name against active accounts

 for active_name in active_names:

 # Fuzzy: check if either contains the other, or significant overlap

 if not company or not active_name:

 continue

 if company in active_name or active_name in company:

 matched_account = active_name

 break

 # Check key words overlap

 company_words = set(company.split())

 active_words = set(active_name.split())

 overlap = company_words & active_words

 # Need substantial overlap (exclude common words)

 meaningful = overlap - {"school", "district", "unified", "elementary", "the", "of", "and", "city", "county"}

 if len(meaningful) >= 2:

 matched_account = active_name

 break

 if matched_account:

 matches.append((name, email, lead.get("Company", ""), matched_account))

 if matches:

 print(f" ⚠️ {len(matches)} leads match active customers:")

 print()

 for name, email, company, account in matches:

 print(f" {name:30s} | {company:45s}")

 print(f" {'':30s} | matched → {account}")

 print()

 else:

 print("✅ No matches found — none of the 58 leads are at active customer accounts.")

 Cross-check CUE leads against Active Accounts

 340 + def _api_post ( path : str , payload : dict ) -> dict :

 341 + """

 342 + Make a POST request to the Outreach API (JSON:API format).

 343 + Returns the JSON response or raises on error.

 344 + """

 345 + url = f " { API_BASE }{path} "

 346 + headers = _get_headers()

 347 +

 348 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 349 + if resp.status_code == 401 :

 350 + if _refresh_access_token():

 351 + headers = _get_headers()

 352 + resp = httpx.post(url, headers = headers, json = payload, timeout = 30.0 )

 353 +

 354 + if resp.status_code not in ( 200 , 201 ):

 355 + body = resp.text[: 500 ]

 356 + logger.error( f "Outreach POST {path} HTTP {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 357 + raise Exception ( f "Outreach API error {resp.status_code} : {body} " )

 358 +

 359 + return resp.json()

 360 +

 361 +

 363 """

 364 Paginate through all results for a GET endpoint.
